file.content:
# Nightly — scheduled build of `main` producing a dated, signed
# pre-release.
#
# Complements `latest.yml`:
#   - `latest` (rolling): rebuilt on every push to main; useful for
#     always-fresh bleeding-edge binaries. Tag is force-replaced.
#   - `nightly-YYYY-MM-DD` (dated): rebuilt once per day at a fixed time;
#     useful when you want a stable reference point to pin to, reproduce
#     bugs against, or bisect regressions over several days.
#
# Retention: only the 7 most recent nightly-* releases are kept so the
# Releases page stays readable.

name: nightly

on:
  schedule:
    # 07:00 UTC = midnight PT, 2am CT, 3am ET. Pick a time when main is
    # least likely to be mid-push.
    - cron: "0 7 * * *"
  workflow_dispatch:

permissions:
  contents: read

concurrency:
  group: nightly
  cancel-in-progress: false

env:
  CARGO_TERM_COLOR: always

jobs:
  prepare:
    name: prepare nightly metadata
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    outputs:
      tag: ${{ steps.meta.outputs.tag }}
      should_run: ${{ steps.meta.outputs.should_run }}
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          fetch-depth: 2
      - name: compute tag + skip-if-no-changes
        id: meta
        env:
          EVENT_NAME: ${{ github.event_name }}
        shell: bash
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          TAG="nightly-$(date -u +%Y-%m-%d)"
          echo "tag=$TAG" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          # On the scheduled run, skip if main hasn't moved in the last 24h
          # (otherwise we rebuild identical bits every day). Manual dispatch
          # always runs.
          if [[ "$EVENT_NAME" == "workflow_dispatch" ]]; then
            echo "should_run=true" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
            exit 0
          fi
          LAST_COMMIT_EPOCH=$(git log -1 --format=%ct origin/main)
          NOW=$(date -u +%s)
          if (( NOW - LAST_COMMIT_EPOCH > 86400 )); then
            echo "no commits in last 24h; skipping nightly"
            echo "should_run=false" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          else
            echo "should_run=true" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          fi

  build-cli:
    name: cli / ${{ matrix.target }}
    needs: prepare
    if: needs.prepare.outputs.should_run == 'true'
    runs-on: ${{ matrix.os }}
    strategy:
      fail-fast: false
      matrix:
        include:
          - { target: x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu,  os: ubuntu-latest,    label: linux-x86_64,  archive: tar.gz }
          - { target: aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu, os: ubuntu-24.04-arm, label: linux-aarch64, archive: tar.gz }
          # macos-latest + cross-compile: macos-13 Intel runners unavailable.
          - { target: x86_64-apple-darwin,       os: macos-latest,     label: macos-x86_64,  archive: tar.gz }
          - { target: aarch64-apple-darwin,      os: macos-latest,     label: macos-aarch64, archive: tar.gz }
          - { target: x86_64-pc-windows-msvc,    os: windows-latest,   label: windows-x86_64, archive: zip }
    env:
      TAG: ${{ needs.prepare.outputs.tag }}
      TARGET: ${{ matrix.target }}
      LABEL: ${{ matrix.label }}
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: jdx/mise-action@v2
        with:
          experimental: true
          install: true
          cache: true
      - shell: bash
        run: rustup target add "$TARGET"
      - uses: Swatinem/rust-cache@v2
        with:
          shared-key: nightly-cli-${{ matrix.target }}

      - shell: bash
        run: cargo build --release --locked --target "$TARGET" -p orpheus-cli -p orpheus-server

      - name: stage artifact (unix)
        if: runner.os != 'Windows'
        shell: bash
        run: |
          NAME="orpheus-$TAG-$LABEL"
          mkdir -p "staging/$NAME"
          cp "target/$TARGET/release/orpheus" "staging/$NAME/"
          cp "target/$TARGET/release/orpheus-server" "staging/$NAME/"
          cp README.md LICENSE "staging/$NAME/"
          ( cd staging && tar -czf "$NAME.tar.gz" "$NAME" )
          echo "ARTIFACT=$NAME" >> "$GITHUB_ENV"

      - name: stage artifact (windows)
        if: runner.os == 'Windows'
        shell: pwsh
        run: |
          $NAME = "orpheus-$env:TAG-$env:LABEL"
          New-Item -ItemType Directory -Force -Path "staging/$NAME"
          Copy-Item "target/$env:TARGET/release/orpheus.exe" "staging/$NAME/"
          Copy-Item "target/$env:TARGET/release/orpheus-server.exe" "staging/$NAME/"
          Copy-Item README.md, LICENSE "staging/$NAME/"
          Compress-Archive -Path "staging/$NAME/*" -DestinationPath "staging/$NAME.zip"
          "ARTIFACT=$NAME" | Out-File -FilePath $env:GITHUB_ENV -Append

      - uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: ${{ env.ARTIFACT }}
          path: staging/${{ env.ARTIFACT }}.${{ matrix.archive }}
          if-no-files-found: error

  build-tauri:
    name: tauri / ${{ matrix.name }}
    needs: prepare
    if: needs.prepare.outputs.should_run == 'true'
    runs-on: ${{ matrix.os }}
    strategy:
      fail-fast: false
      matrix:
        include:
          - { name: macos-universal, os: macos-latest,   args: "--target universal-apple-darwin", rust-targets: "aarch64-apple-darwin,x86_64-apple-darwin" }
          - { name: linux-x86_64,    os: ubuntu-22.04,   args: "",                                rust-targets: "" }
          - { name: windows-x86_64,  os: windows-latest, args: "",                                rust-targets: "" }
    env:
      RUST_TARGETS: ${{ matrix.rust-targets }}
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: jdx/mise-action@v2
        with:
          experimental: true
          install: true
          cache: true
      - name: add rust targets
        if: matrix.rust-targets != ''
        shell: bash
        run: |
          IFS=',' read -r -a targets <<< "$RUST_TARGETS"
          for t in "${targets[@]}"; do rustup target add "$t"; done
      - uses: Swatinem/rust-cache@v2
        with:
          workspaces: "crates/orpheus-tauri/src-tauri -> target"
          shared-key: nightly-tauri-${{ matrix.name }}

      - uses: ./.github/actions/install-linux-deps

      - run: mise run web:install
      - uses: tauri-apps/tauri-action@v0
        env:
          GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
        with:
          projectPath: crates/orpheus-tauri/src-tauri
          args: ${{ matrix.args }}

      - name: collect tauri bundles
        shell: bash
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          mkdir -p tauri-output
          # Cargo workspace: bundles land under the workspace root `target/`.
          # Layout varies by target triple:
          #   native   -> target/release/bundle/<kind>/...
          #   crossed  -> target/<triple>/release/bundle/<kind>/...
          #               (e.g. target/universal-apple-darwin/release/bundle/...)
          # So search the whole target/ tree and filter by the
          # .../release/bundle/ path segment. Tauri v2 emits duplicates
          # (Orpheus_0.1.0_amd64.deb AND lowercase
          # orpheus_0.1.0_amd64.deb from the Cargo crate name); the
          # Orpheus_/Orpheus- name filter keeps only the productName-cased
          # ones.
          find target -type f -path "*/release/bundle/*" \
            \( -name "Orpheus_*.dmg"    -o -name "Orpheus*.app.tar.gz" \
               -o -name "Orpheus_*.AppImage" \
               -o -name "Orpheus_*.deb" -o -name "Orpheus-*.rpm" \
               -o -name "Orpheus_*.msi" -o -name "Orpheus_*.exe" \) \
            -exec cp {} tauri-output/ \;
          ls -la tauri-output/
          if [ -z "$(ls -A tauri-output/)" ]; then
            echo "::error::no Tauri bundles found under target/**/release/bundle"
            find target -type d -name bundle 2>&1 | head -20 || true
            exit 1
          fi

      - uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: tauri-${{ matrix.name }}
          path: tauri-output/*
          if-no-files-found: error

  publish:
    name: publish nightly pre-release
    needs: [prepare, build-cli, build-tauri]
    if: needs.prepare.outputs.should_run == 'true'
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      contents: write
    env:
      TAG: ${{ needs.prepare.outputs.tag }}
      SHA: ${{ github.sha }}
      RUN_ID: ${{ github.run_id }}
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0

      - uses: actions/download-artifact@v4
        with:
          path: dist
          merge-multiple: true

      - name: list artifacts
        run: ls -la dist/

      - name: sign artifacts with gpg
        uses: ./.github/actions/sign-artifacts
        with:
          artifacts-dir: dist
          gpg-private-key: ${{ secrets.GPG_PRIVATE_KEY }}
          gpg-passphrase: ${{ secrets.GPG_PASSPHRASE }}
          gpg-key-id: ${{ secrets.GPG_KEY_ID }}

      - name: delete prior nightly release with same date (re-run safety)
        env:
          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
        shell: bash
        run: gh release delete "$TAG" --yes --cleanup-tag || true

      - name: create nightly pre-release
        uses: softprops/action-gh-release@v2
        with:
          tag_name: ${{ env.TAG }}
          name: "Nightly ${{ env.TAG }}"
          prerelease: true
          make_latest: "false"
          body: |
            **Scheduled nightly build from `main`.** Dated and immutable —
            a new nightly is cut once per day. For a stable version, use a
            tagged release.

            - date:   `${{ env.TAG }}`
            - commit: `${{ github.sha }}`
            - run:    `${{ github.run_id }}`

            All artifacts are GPG-signed. Verify with:
            ```
            gpg --verify SHA256SUMS.asc SHA256SUMS
            sha256sum -c SHA256SUMS
            ```
          files: dist/*
          fail_on_unmatched_files: true

      - name: prune old nightly releases (keep last 7)
        env:
          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
        shell: bash
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          # List nightly-* releases sorted newest-first, skip the 7 most recent,
          # delete the rest.
          gh release list --limit 200 --json tagName,createdAt \
            --jq '[.[] | select(.tagName | startswith("nightly-"))] | sort_by(.createdAt) | reverse | .[7:] | .[].tagName' \
            | while read -r OLD; do
                [ -z "$OLD" ] && continue
                echo "deleting old nightly: $OLD"
                gh release delete "$OLD" --yes --cleanup-tag || true
              done

file.content:
# Publish crates to crates.io — called by release.yml after a tagged
# release is live on GitHub.
#
# Only publishes library + binary crates that have `publish = true`
# (the default). `orpheus-tauri` and `orpheus-demo-fixtures` are marked
# `publish = false` and skipped automatically by cargo.
#
# Order matters: orpheus-core must publish before orpheus-cli and
# orpheus-server because those depend on it via path + version. Cargo
# usually discovers this via `cargo publish --workspace`, but we publish
# explicitly for predictability and better error reporting.

name: publish-crates

on:
  workflow_call:
    inputs:
      tag:
        description: "Release tag (e.g. v0.1.1). The crate version must match tag-less."
        required: true
        type: string
  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      tag:
        description: "Release tag (e.g. v0.1.1)."
        required: true
        type: string
      dry_run:
        description: "If true, run cargo publish --dry-run only."
        required: false
        type: boolean
        default: false

permissions:
  contents: read

concurrency:
  group: publish-crates-${{ inputs.tag }}
  cancel-in-progress: false

jobs:
  publish:
    name: cargo publish
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    env:
      TAG: ${{ inputs.tag }}
      DRY_RUN: ${{ inputs.dry_run || 'false' }}
      CARGO_REGISTRY_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.CARGO_REGISTRY_TOKEN }}
    steps:
      - name: check CARGO_REGISTRY_TOKEN is set
        id: gate
        env:
          TOKEN: ${{ secrets.CARGO_REGISTRY_TOKEN }}
        shell: bash
        run: |
          if [[ -z "$TOKEN" ]]; then
            echo "::warning::CARGO_REGISTRY_TOKEN not set; skipping crates.io publish"
            echo "skip=true" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          else
            echo "skip=false" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          fi

      - if: steps.gate.outputs.skip == 'false'
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          ref: ${{ inputs.tag }}
          fetch-depth: 0

      - if: steps.gate.outputs.skip == 'false'
        uses: jdx/mise-action@v2
        with:
          experimental: true
          install: true
          cache: true

      - if: steps.gate.outputs.skip == 'false'
        uses: Swatinem/rust-cache@v2
        with:
          shared-key: publish-crates

      - if: steps.gate.outputs.skip == 'false'
        name: verify crate versions match the tag
        shell: bash
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          EXPECTED="${TAG#v}"
          WORKSPACE_VERSION=$(grep -E '^version\s*=' Cargo.toml | head -1 | sed -E 's/.*"([^"]+)".*/\1/')
          echo "tag: $TAG  -> expected crate version: $EXPECTED"
          echo "workspace version: $WORKSPACE_VERSION"
          if [[ "$EXPECTED" != "$WORKSPACE_VERSION" ]]; then
            echo "::error::tag ($EXPECTED) does not match workspace.version ($WORKSPACE_VERSION)"
            exit 1
          fi

      - name: cargo publish (dry-run)
        if: steps.gate.outputs.skip == 'false' && env.DRY_RUN == 'true'
        shell: bash
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          for c in orpheus-core orpheus-cli orpheus-server; do
            echo "=== dry-run: $c ==="
            cargo publish --dry-run -p "$c"
          done

      - name: cargo publish (real)
        if: steps.gate.outputs.skip == 'false' && env.DRY_RUN != 'true'
        shell: bash
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          # Publish in dependency order. Between crates, pause briefly to let
          # the crates.io index catch up so dependents can resolve the new
          # version. `--no-verify` on dependents avoids a redundant full build.
          publish_one() {
            local crate=$1
            shift
            echo "=== publishing: $crate ==="
            # Allow failure only for the specific "already uploaded" case so
            # re-runs are idempotent.
            if ! cargo publish -p "$crate" "$@"; then
              if cargo search "$crate" | grep -q "^$crate = \"${TAG#v}\""; then
                echo "$crate@${TAG#v} already on crates.io; skipping"
                return 0
              fi
              return 1
            fi
          }
          publish_one orpheus-core
          # Give crates.io a moment to index the new version.
          sleep 30
          publish_one orpheus-cli --no-verify
          publish_one orpheus-server --no-verify

.github/workflows/publish-cask-head.yml
.github/workflows/publish-cask.yml

1	# Publish Homebrew HEAD cask — called by latest.yml after the rolling
2	# `latest` pre-release is atomically replaced on every push to main.
3	#
4	# Writes `Casks/orpheus-head.rb` to the external tap repo
5	# `dougnukem/homebrew-orpheus` so users can
6	# `brew install --cask dougnukem/orpheus/orpheus-head` and receive a
7	# fresh rolling build with every `brew reinstall --cask orpheus-head`.
8	#
9	# The head cask uses `version :latest` + `sha256 :no_check` because the
10	# artifact hash changes on every push to main. Users opt into this
11	# instability explicitly by installing the `-head` variant.
12	#
13	# Requires:
14	#   - HOMEBREW_TAP_TOKEN secret (GitHub PAT with repo scope on the tap)
15	#   - the tap repo to already exist (bootstrapped by publish-cask.yml on
16	#     the first tagged release). If it does not exist yet this workflow
17	#     will fail — run publish-cask.yml first, or create the repo by hand.
18	#
19	# Security: all ${{ github.* }} inputs are routed through env: blocks and
20	# referenced as shell variables. The workflow only uses trusted context
21	# values (github.repository, github.sha) — no untrusted event payload.
22	
23	name: publish-cask-head
24	
25	on:
26	  workflow_call: {}
27	  workflow_dispatch: {}
28	
29	permissions:
30	  contents: read
31	
32	concurrency:
33	  group: publish-cask-head
34	  cancel-in-progress: false
35	
36	env:
37	  TAP_OWNER: dougnukem
38	  TAP_REPO: homebrew-orpheus
39	  LATEST_TAG: latest
40	
41	jobs:
42	  update-cask-head:
43	    name: update homebrew head cask
44	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
45	    env:
46	      REPO: ${{ github.repository }}
47	      SHA: ${{ github.sha }}
48	    steps:
49	      - name: check HOMEBREW_TAP_TOKEN is set
50	        id: gate
51	        env:
52	          TAP_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.HOMEBREW_TAP_TOKEN }}
53	        shell: bash
54	        run: |
55	          if [[ -z "$TAP_TOKEN" ]]; then
56	            echo "::warning::HOMEBREW_TAP_TOKEN not set; skipping head cask publish"
57	            echo "skip=true" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
58	          else
59	            echo "skip=false" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
60	          fi
61	
62	      - if: steps.gate.outputs.skip == 'false'
63	        uses: actions/checkout@v4
64	
65	      - if: steps.gate.outputs.skip == 'false'
66	        name: locate universal dmg on latest release
67	        id: dmg
68	        env:
69	          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
70	        shell: bash
71	        run: |
72	          set -euo pipefail
73	          DMG=$(gh release view "$LATEST_TAG" --repo "$REPO" \
74	            --json assets --jq '.assets[].name' \
75	            | grep -E '^Orpheus_.*_universal\.dmg$' \
76	            | head -n1 || true)
77	          if [[ -z "$DMG" ]]; then
78	            echo "::error::no Orpheus_*_universal.dmg asset on $LATEST_TAG release"
79	            exit 1
80	          fi
81	          echo "dmg=$DMG" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
82	
83	      - if: steps.gate.outputs.skip == 'false'
84	        name: checkout tap repo
85	        uses: actions/checkout@v4
86	        with:
87	          repository: ${{ env.TAP_OWNER }}/${{ env.TAP_REPO }}
88	          token: ${{ secrets.HOMEBREW_TAP_TOKEN }}
89	          path: tap
90	
91	      - if: steps.gate.outputs.skip == 'false'
92	        name: render head cask from template
93	        env:
94	          DMG: ${{ steps.dmg.outputs.dmg }}
95	        shell: bash
96	        run: |
97	          set -euo pipefail
98	          mkdir -p tap/Casks
99	          sed \
100	            -e "s|%%REPO%%|$REPO|g" \
101	            -e "s|%%DMG_FILENAME%%|$DMG|g" \
102	            -e "s|%%TAP_OWNER%%|$TAP_OWNER|g" \
103	            -e "s|%%TAP_NAME%%|orpheus|g" \
104	            packaging/homebrew/orpheus-head.rb.template > tap/Casks/orpheus-head.rb
105	          cat tap/Casks/orpheus-head.rb
106	
107	      - if: steps.gate.outputs.skip == 'false'
108	        name: commit + push tap update
109	        shell: bash
110	        working-directory: tap
111	        run: |
112	          set -euo pipefail
113	          git config user.name "orpheus-release-bot"
114	          git config user.email "bot@users.noreply.github.com"
115	          # Stage first so a previously-untracked file registers as a
116	          # change; then diff against the index. `git diff --quiet` on
117	          # its own misses new files and silently exits clean.
118	          git add Casks/orpheus-head.rb
119	          if git diff --cached --quiet; then
120	            echo "head cask already up to date; nothing to commit"
121	            exit 0
122	          fi
123	          git commit -m "orpheus-head @ ${SHA:0:7}"
124	          git push
125

file.content:
# Publish Homebrew HEAD cask — called by latest.yml after the rolling
# `latest` pre-release is atomically replaced on every push to main.
#
# Writes `Casks/orpheus-head.rb` to the external tap repo
# `dougnukem/homebrew-orpheus` so users can
# `brew install --cask dougnukem/orpheus/orpheus-head` and receive a
# fresh rolling build with every `brew reinstall --cask orpheus-head`.
#
# The head cask uses `version :latest` + `sha256 :no_check` because the
# artifact hash changes on every push to main. Users opt into this
# instability explicitly by installing the `-head` variant.
#
# Requires:
#   - HOMEBREW_TAP_TOKEN secret (GitHub PAT with repo scope on the tap)
#   - the tap repo to already exist (bootstrapped by publish-cask.yml on
#     the first tagged release). If it does not exist yet this workflow
#     will fail — run publish-cask.yml first, or create the repo by hand.
#
# Security: all ${{ github.* }} inputs are routed through env: blocks and
# referenced as shell variables. The workflow only uses trusted context
# values (github.repository, github.sha) — no untrusted event payload.

name: publish-cask-head

on:
  workflow_call: {}
  workflow_dispatch: {}

permissions:
  contents: read

concurrency:
  group: publish-cask-head
  cancel-in-progress: false

env:
  TAP_OWNER: dougnukem
  TAP_REPO: homebrew-orpheus
  LATEST_TAG: latest

jobs:
  update-cask-head:
    name: update homebrew head cask
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    env:
      REPO: ${{ github.repository }}
      SHA: ${{ github.sha }}
    steps:
      - name: check HOMEBREW_TAP_TOKEN is set
        id: gate
        env:
          TAP_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.HOMEBREW_TAP_TOKEN }}
        shell: bash
        run: |
          if [[ -z "$TAP_TOKEN" ]]; then
            echo "::warning::HOMEBREW_TAP_TOKEN not set; skipping head cask publish"
            echo "skip=true" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          else
            echo "skip=false" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          fi

      - if: steps.gate.outputs.skip == 'false'
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - if: steps.gate.outputs.skip == 'false'
        name: locate universal dmg on latest release
        id: dmg
        env:
          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
        shell: bash
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          DMG=$(gh release view "$LATEST_TAG" --repo "$REPO" \
            --json assets --jq '.assets[].name' \
            | grep -E '^Orpheus_.*_universal\.dmg$' \
            | head -n1 || true)
          if [[ -z "$DMG" ]]; then
            echo "::error::no Orpheus_*_universal.dmg asset on $LATEST_TAG release"
            exit 1
          fi
          echo "dmg=$DMG" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"

      - if: steps.gate.outputs.skip == 'false'
        name: checkout tap repo
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          repository: ${{ env.TAP_OWNER }}/${{ env.TAP_REPO }}
          token: ${{ secrets.HOMEBREW_TAP_TOKEN }}
          path: tap

      - if: steps.gate.outputs.skip == 'false'
        name: render head cask from template
        env:
          DMG: ${{ steps.dmg.outputs.dmg }}
        shell: bash
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          mkdir -p tap/Casks
          sed \
            -e "s|%%REPO%%|$REPO|g" \
            -e "s|%%DMG_FILENAME%%|$DMG|g" \
            -e "s|%%TAP_OWNER%%|$TAP_OWNER|g" \
            -e "s|%%TAP_NAME%%|orpheus|g" \
            packaging/homebrew/orpheus-head.rb.template > tap/Casks/orpheus-head.rb
          cat tap/Casks/orpheus-head.rb

      - if: steps.gate.outputs.skip == 'false'
        name: commit + push tap update
        shell: bash
        working-directory: tap
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          git config user.name "orpheus-release-bot"
          git config user.email "bot@users.noreply.github.com"
          # Stage first so a previously-untracked file registers as a
          # change; then diff against the index. `git diff --quiet` on
          # its own misses new files and silently exits clean.
          git add Casks/orpheus-head.rb
          if git diff --cached --quiet; then
            echo "head cask already up to date; nothing to commit"
            exit 0
          fi
          git commit -m "orpheus-head @ ${SHA:0:7}"
          git push

content.content:
1	# Publish Homebrew cask — called by release.yml after a tagged release
2	# is live on GitHub.
3	#
4	# Writes an updated Cask formula to the external tap repository
5	# `dougnukem/homebrew-orpheus` so users can `brew install --cask orpheus`
6	# and receive the newest version.
7	#
8	# Requires:
9	#   - a HOMEBREW_TAP_TOKEN secret (GitHub PAT with repo scope on the tap)
10	#   - the tap repo to already exist and contain Casks/orpheus.rb (even
11	#     empty). The first version is bootstrapped by hand; subsequent
12	#     releases are updated automatically by this workflow.
13	#
14	# macOS signing status:
15	#   * The Tauri .app produced by release.yml is currently UNSIGNED. The
16	#     cask installs successfully but users hit Gatekeeper on first launch.
17	#   * The cask template below includes Gatekeeper-bypass instructions in
18	#     its `caveats` block.
19	#   * Once Apple Developer signing is enabled in release.yml, delete the
20	#     caveats block in packaging/homebrew/orpheus.rb.template and re-run.
21	
22	name: publish-cask
23	
24	on:
25	  workflow_call:
26	    inputs:
27	      tag:
28	        description: "Release tag (e.g. v0.1.1)."
29	        required: true
30	        type: string
31	  workflow_dispatch:
32	    inputs:
33	      tag:
34	        description: "Release tag (e.g. v0.1.1)."
35	        required: true
36	        type: string
37	
38	permissions:
39	  contents: read
40	
41	concurrency:
42	  group: publish-cask-${{ inputs.tag }}
43	  cancel-in-progress: false
44	
45	env:
46	  TAP_OWNER: dougnukem
47	  TAP_REPO: homebrew-orpheus
48	
49	jobs:

file.content:
# Publish Homebrew cask — called by release.yml after a tagged release
# is live on GitHub.
#
# Writes an updated Cask formula to the external tap repository
# `dougnukem/homebrew-orpheus` so users can `brew install --cask orpheus`
# and receive the newest version.
#
# Requires:
#   - a HOMEBREW_TAP_TOKEN secret (GitHub PAT with repo scope on the tap)
#   - the tap repo to already exist and contain Casks/orpheus.rb (even
#     empty). The first version is bootstrapped by hand; subsequent
#     releases are updated automatically by this workflow.
#
# macOS signing status:
#   * The Tauri .app produced by release.yml is currently UNSIGNED. The
#     cask installs successfully but users hit Gatekeeper on first launch.
#   * The cask template below includes Gatekeeper-bypass instructions in
#     its `caveats` block.
#   * Once Apple Developer signing is enabled in release.yml, delete the
#     caveats block in packaging/homebrew/orpheus.rb.template and re-run.

name: publish-cask

on:
  workflow_call:
    inputs:
      tag:
        description: "Release tag (e.g. v0.1.1)."
        required: true
        type: string
  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      tag:
        description: "Release tag (e.g. v0.1.1)."
        required: true
        type: string

permissions:
  contents: read

concurrency:
  group: publish-cask-${{ inputs.tag }}
  cancel-in-progress: false

env:
  TAP_OWNER: dougnukem
  TAP_REPO: homebrew-orpheus

jobs:
  update-cask:
    name: update homebrew cask
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    env:
      TAG: ${{ inputs.tag }}
      REPO: ${{ github.repository }}
    steps:
      - name: check HOMEBREW_TAP_TOKEN is set
        id: gate
        env:
          TAP_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.HOMEBREW_TAP_TOKEN }}
        shell: bash
        run: |
          if [[ -z "$TAP_TOKEN" ]]; then
            echo "::warning::HOMEBREW_TAP_TOKEN not set; skipping cask publish"
            echo "skip=true" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          else
            echo "skip=false" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          fi

      - if: steps.gate.outputs.skip == 'false'
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          ref: ${{ inputs.tag }}

      - if: steps.gate.outputs.skip == 'false'
        name: derive version
        id: ver
        shell: bash
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          VERSION="${TAG#v}"
          echo "version=$VERSION" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"

      - if: steps.gate.outputs.skip == 'false'
        name: compute artifact sha256s
        id: sha
        env:
          VERSION: ${{ steps.ver.outputs.version }}
        shell: bash
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          BASE="https://github.com/$REPO/releases/download/$TAG"
          # Tauri produces per-arch DMGs named like:
          #   Orpheus_0.1.0_aarch64.dmg
          #   Orpheus_0.1.0_x64.dmg
          # The universal-apple-darwin target combines both architectures
          # into a single DMG labelled universal. Fetch whichever exists.
          fetch_sha() {
            local url=$1
            if curl -sfL -o /tmp/art "$url"; then
              sha256sum /tmp/art | cut -d' ' -f1
            else
              echo "MISSING"
            fi
          }
          ARM_SHA=$(fetch_sha "$BASE/Orpheus_${VERSION}_aarch64.dmg")
          INTEL_SHA=$(fetch_sha "$BASE/Orpheus_${VERSION}_x64.dmg")
          UNIV_SHA=$(fetch_sha "$BASE/Orpheus_${VERSION}_universal.dmg")
          echo "arm=$ARM_SHA"        >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          echo "intel=$INTEL_SHA"    >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          echo "universal=$UNIV_SHA" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"

      - if: steps.gate.outputs.skip == 'false'
        name: checkout tap repo
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          repository: ${{ env.TAP_OWNER }}/${{ env.TAP_REPO }}
          token: ${{ secrets.HOMEBREW_TAP_TOKEN }}
          path: tap

      - if: steps.gate.outputs.skip == 'false'
        name: render cask from template
        env:
          VERSION: ${{ steps.ver.outputs.version }}
          ARM_SHA: ${{ steps.sha.outputs.arm }}
          INTEL_SHA: ${{ steps.sha.outputs.intel }}
          UNIV_SHA: ${{ steps.sha.outputs.universal }}
        shell: bash
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          mkdir -p tap/Casks
          if [[ "$UNIV_SHA" != "MISSING" ]]; then
            # Single-SHA universal binary variant.
            sed \
              -e "s|%%VERSION%%|$VERSION|g" \
              -e "s|%%REPO%%|$REPO|g" \
              -e "s|%%UNIVERSAL_SHA%%|$UNIV_SHA|g" \
              packaging/homebrew/orpheus.rb.universal.template > tap/Casks/orpheus.rb
          else
            if [[ "$ARM_SHA" == "MISSING" || "$INTEL_SHA" == "MISSING" ]]; then
              echo "::error::neither universal nor both per-arch DMGs present for $TAG"
              exit 1
            fi
            sed \
              -e "s|%%VERSION%%|$VERSION|g" \
              -e "s|%%REPO%%|$REPO|g" \
              -e "s|%%ARM_SHA%%|$ARM_SHA|g" \
              -e "s|%%INTEL_SHA%%|$INTEL_SHA|g" \
              packaging/homebrew/orpheus.rb.perarch.template > tap/Casks/orpheus.rb
          fi
          cat tap/Casks/orpheus.rb

      - if: steps.gate.outputs.skip == 'false'
        name: commit + push tap update
        env:
          VERSION: ${{ steps.ver.outputs.version }}
        shell: bash
        working-directory: tap
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          git config user.name "orpheus-release-bot"
          git config user.email "bot@users.noreply.github.com"
          # Stage first so a previously-untracked file registers as a
          # change; then diff against the index. `git diff --quiet` on
          # its own misses new files and silently exits clean.
          git add Casks/orpheus.rb
          if git diff --cached --quiet; then
            echo "cask already up to date; nothing to commit"
            exit 0
          fi
          git commit -m "orpheus ${VERSION}"
          git push

[package]
name = "orpheus-cli"
version.workspace = true
edition.workspace = true
rust-version.workspace = true
license.workspace = true
authors.workspace = true
repository.workspace = true
homepage.workspace = true
description = "Orpheus command-line interface — scan, extract, serve, and recover Bitcoin wallets"
readme = "../../README.md"
keywords = ["bitcoin", "wallet", "recovery", "cli", "bip39"]
categories = ["command-line-utilities", "cryptography::cryptocurrencies"]

[[bin]]
name = "orpheus"
path = "src/main.rs"

[package.metadata.binstall]
pkg-url = "{ repo }/releases/download/{ version }/orpheus-{ version }-{ target }{ archive-suffix }"
pkg-fmt = "tgz"
bin-dir = "orpheus-{ version }-{ target }/{ bin }{ binary-ext }"

[package.metadata.binstall.overrides.x86_64-pc-windows-msvc]
pkg-fmt = "zip"

[dependencies]
orpheus-core = { path = "../orpheus-core", version = "0.1.0" }
anyhow.workspace = true
clap.workspace = true
comfy-table.workspace = true
serde.workspace = true
serde_json.workspace = true
tracing.workspace = true
tracing-subscriber.workspace = true

# Async runtime + web server for `orpheus serve`, which hosts the embedded
# React UI (apps/web/dist) and JSON API from the same binary.
tokio.workspace = true
axum.workspace = true
tower.workspace = true
tower-http.workspace = true
rust-embed.workspace = true
mime_guess.workspace = true
tempfile = "3"

[[bin]]
name = "orpheus"
path = "src/main.rs"

[package.metadata.binstall]
pkg-url = "{ repo }/releases/download/{ version }/orpheus-{ version }-{ target }{ archive-suffix }"
pkg-fmt = "tgz"
bin-dir = "orpheus-{ version }-{ target }/{ bin }{ binary-ext }"

[package.metadata.binstall.overrides.x86_64-pc-windows-msvc]
pkg-fmt = "zip"

[dependencies]
orpheus-core = { path = "../orpheus-core", version = "0.1.0" }
anyhow.workspace = true
clap.workspace = true
comfy-table.workspace = true
serde_json.workspace = true
tracing.workspace = true
tracing-subscriber.workspace = true
tokio.workspace = true

[workspace]
resolver = "3"
members = [
    "crates/orpheus-core",
    "crates/orpheus-cli",
    "crates/orpheus-server",
    "crates/orpheus-tauri/src-tauri",
    "crates/orpheus-demo-fixtures",
]

[workspace.package]
version = "0.1.0"
edition = "2024"
rust-version = "1.89"
license = "MIT"
authors = ["Orpheus Contributors"]
repository = "https://github.com/dougnukem/orpheus"
homepage = "https://github.com/dougnukem/orpheus"
description = "Recover lost cryptocurrency from forgotten wallets"

[workspace.dependencies]
# Crypto — rust-bitcoin ecosystem (v0.32 is current stable as of 2026-04)
bitcoin = { version = "0.32", features = ["serde", "rand-std"] }
bip39 = { version = "2.1", features = ["rand"] }
secp256k1 = { version = "0.29", features = ["global-context", "rand-std"] }
scrypt = { version = "0.11" }
aes = "0.8"
cbc = { version = "0.1", features = ["std"] }
sha2 = "0.10"
ripemd = "0.1"
hmac = "0.12"
pbkdf2 = "0.12"
hex = "0.4"
base58 = "0.2"
regex = "1.11"

# Runtime / web
tokio = { version = "1.42", features = ["full"] }
axum = { version = "0.8", features = ["multipart", "macros"] }
tower = "0.5"
tower-http = { version = "0.6", features = ["trace", "cors", "fs"] }
rust-embed = { version = "8.5", features = ["mime-guess"] }
mime_guess = "2"

# Serde / errors / logging / fs
serde = { version = "1", features = ["derive"] }
serde_json = "1"
thiserror = "2"
anyhow = "1"
tracing = "0.1"
tracing-subscriber = { version = "0.3", features = ["env-filter"] }
walkdir = "2"

# CLI
clap = { version = "4.5", features = ["derive", "env"] }
comfy-table = "7.1"
indicatif = "0.17"

# HTTP client for balance providers
reqwest = { version = "0.12", features = ["json", "rustls-tls", "blocking"], default-features = false }

[profile.release]
lto = "thin"
codegen-units = 1
strip = true

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Orpheus — developer environment managed by mise (https://mise.jdx.dev).
#
# mise is the single source of truth for tool versions and for every
# scripted task. The same `mise run <task>` commands are invoked from
# local shells, git hooks, and CI (see .github/workflows/*.yml).
#
# On `cd` into the repo (with mise's shell integration active) the pinned
# toolchain versions auto-activate. Run `mise install` to fetch them the
# first time.

[tools]
rust = "1.95"
node = "22"
pnpm = "9"
# Tauri build prerequisites on macOS are Xcode Command Line Tools; on Linux
# users need libwebkit2gtk-4.1-dev + friends (see .github/workflows/ci.yml).

[env]
# rustup-managed toolchain always wins over /opt/homebrew/bin/{cargo,rustc}.
# Windows CI runners don't set $HOME — fall back to %USERPROFILE% there so
# mise can still render the template.
_.path = [
    "{% if env.HOME %}{{ env.HOME }}/.cargo/bin{% elif env.USERPROFILE %}{{ env.USERPROFILE }}/.cargo/bin{% else %}{{ config_root }}/.cargo/bin{% endif %}",
]
RUST_BACKTRACE = "1"
RUST_LOG       = "orpheus=info,tower_http=info"

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Lifecycle hooks
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# "enter" fires once per shell when mise activates this project. We use it
# to install git hooks on the first entry of a fresh clone.
[hooks]
enter = "test -x .git/hooks/pre-commit || mise run hooks:install"

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Setup / housekeeping
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

[tasks.setup]
description = "Install all Rust + frontend dependencies"
run = [
    "cargo fetch --locked || cargo fetch",
    "mise run web:install",
]

[tasks."hooks:install"]
description = "Symlink git hooks from .githooks/ into .git/hooks/"
run = """
set -e
mkdir -p .git/hooks
for hook in .githooks/*; do
    name=$(basename "$hook")
    ln -sf "../../$hook" ".git/hooks/$name"
done
echo "git hooks installed from .githooks/"
"""

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Rust
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

[tasks.build]
description = "Build the whole Rust workspace (debug)"
run = "cargo build --workspace --all-targets"

[tasks."build:release"]
description = "Release build of the CLI + server binaries into target/release"
run = "cargo build --release --locked -p orpheus-cli -p orpheus-server"

[tasks.install]
description = "Install `orpheus` + `orpheus-server` into ~/.cargo/bin"
run = "cargo install --path crates/orpheus-cli --locked --force && cargo install --path crates/orpheus-server --locked --force"

[tasks.test]
description = "Run all Rust tests"
# orpheus-tauri has no tests of its own. clippy --workspace compile-checks
# it and latest.yml handles the full tauri build for releases.
run = "cargo test --workspace --locked --exclude orpheus-tauri"

[tasks."test:core"]
description = "Run orpheus-core tests only"
run = "cargo test -p orpheus-core --locked"

[tasks."rust:lint"]
description = "Cargo fmt --check + clippy"
run = [
    "cargo fmt --all -- --check",
    "cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings",
]

[tasks."rust:fmt"]
description = "Auto-format Rust sources"
run = "cargo fmt --all"

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Frontend (apps/web)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

[tasks."web:install"]
description = "Install apps/web dependencies with pnpm"
dir = "apps/web"
run = "pnpm install --frozen-lockfile || pnpm install"

[tasks."web:dev"]
description = "Vite dev server on :5173 proxying /api -> :3000"
dir = "apps/web"
run = "pnpm dev"

[tasks."web:build"]
description = "Build React app into apps/web/dist"
dir = "apps/web"
run = "pnpm build"

[tasks."web:lint"]
description = "Run ESLint on the frontend"
depends = ["web:install"]
dir = "apps/web"
run = "pnpm lint"

[tasks."web:typecheck"]
description = "Run the TS compiler without emitting"
depends = ["web:install"]
dir = "apps/web"
run = "pnpm typecheck"

[tasks."web:fmt"]
description = "Prettier write for frontend sources"
dir = "apps/web"
run = "pnpm format"

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Composite / meta tasks
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

[tasks.lint]
description = "Full lint: Rust fmt/clippy + frontend lint + typecheck"
depends = ["rust:lint", "web:lint", "web:typecheck"]

[tasks.fmt]
description = "Auto-format everything"
depends = ["rust:fmt", "web:fmt"]

[tasks.pre-commit]
description = "Fast checks for the git pre-commit hook"
depends = ["rust:lint"]

[tasks.ci]
description = "What CI runs on every pull request"
depends = ["lint", "test", "web:build", "cli:demo"]

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Running the app
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

[tasks."server:dev"]
description = "Axum server on 127.0.0.1:3000 serving embedded web/dist"
run = "cargo run -p orpheus-server -- --host 127.0.0.1 --port 3000"

[tasks.dev]
description = "Run axum (:3000) + Vite (:5173) together"
run = [
    { task = "server:dev" },
    { task = "web:dev" },
]

[tasks."cli:demo"]
description = "Run the bundled offline demo scan (synthetic fixtures)"
run = "cargo run -p orpheus-cli --locked -- demo"

[tasks."demo:fixtures"]
description = "Regenerate synthetic demo wallet fixtures"
run = "cargo run -p orpheus-demo-fixtures --locked"

[tasks."tauri:dev"]
description = "Tauri v2 desktop app in development mode"
dir = "crates/orpheus-tauri"
run = "pnpm tauri dev"

[tasks."tauri:build"]
description = "Package the Tauri desktop app for the current platform"
dir = "crates/orpheus-tauri"
run = "pnpm tauri build"

[tasks."release:setup-gpg"]
description = "One-time: generate the Orpheus release signing key + sync to GitHub secrets + .secrets"
# raw = true preserves the controlling TTY so the script's silent
# passphrase prompt (read -s) works.
raw = true
run = "./scripts/setup-gpg-release-key.sh"

+description = "Release build of the unified `orpheus` binary into target/release"

[tasks."server:dev"]
description = "`orpheus serve` on 127.0.0.1:3000 serving embedded web/dist + /api"
run = "cargo run -p orpheus-cli -- serve --host 127.0.0.1 --port 3000"
toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Orpheus — developer environment managed by mise (https://mise.jdx.dev).
#
# mise is the single source of truth for tool versions and for every
# scripted task. The same `mise run <task>` commands are invoked from
# local shells, git hooks, and CI (see .github/workflows/*.yml).
#
# On `cd` into the repo (with mise's shell integration active) the pinned
# toolchain versions auto-activate. Run `mise install` to fetch them the
# first time.

[tools]
rust = "1.95"
node = "22"
pnpm = "9"
# Tauri build prerequisites on macOS are Xcode Command Line Tools; on Linux
# users need libwebkit2gtk-4.1-dev + friends (see .github/workflows/ci.yml).

[env]
# rustup-managed toolchain always wins over /opt/homebrew/bin/{cargo,rustc}.
# Windows CI runners don't set $HOME — fall back to %USERPROFILE% there so
# mise can still render the template.
_.path = [
    "{% if env.HOME %}{{ env.HOME }}/.cargo/bin{% elif env.USERPROFILE %}{{ env.USERPROFILE }}/.cargo/bin{% else %}{{ config_root }}/.cargo/bin{% endif %}",
]
RUST_BACKTRACE = "1"
RUST_LOG       = "orpheus=info,tower_http=info"

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Lifecycle hooks
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# "enter" fires once per shell when mise activates this project. We use it
# to install git hooks on the first entry of a fresh clone.
[hooks]
enter = "test -x .git/hooks/pre-commit || mise run hooks:install"

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Setup / housekeeping
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

[tasks.setup]
description = "Install all Rust + frontend dependencies"
run = [
    "cargo fetch --locked || cargo fetch",
    "mise run web:install",
]

[tasks."hooks:install"]
description = "Symlink git hooks from .githooks/ into .git/hooks/"
run = """
set -e
mkdir -p .git/hooks
for hook in .githooks/*; do
    name=$(basename "$hook")
    ln -sf "../../$hook" ".git/hooks/$name"
done
echo "git hooks installed from .githooks/"
"""

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Rust
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

[tasks.build]
description = "Build the whole Rust workspace (debug)"
run = "cargo build --workspace --all-targets"

[tasks."build:release"]
description = "Release build of the unified `orpheus` binary into target/release"
run = "cargo build --release --locked -p orpheus-cli"

[tasks.install]
description = "Install `orpheus` into ~/.cargo/bin"
run = "cargo install --path crates/orpheus-cli --locked --force"

[tasks.test]
description = "Run all Rust tests"
# orpheus-tauri has no tests of its own. clippy --workspace compile-checks
# it and latest.yml handles the full tauri build for releases.
run = "cargo test --workspace --locked --exclude orpheus-tauri"

[tasks."test:core"]
description = "Run orpheus-core tests only"
run = "cargo test -p orpheus-core --locked"

[tasks."rust:lint"]
description = "Cargo fmt --check + clippy"
run = [
    "cargo fmt --all -- --check",
    "cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings",
]

[tasks."rust:fmt"]
description = "Auto-format Rust sources"
run = "cargo fmt --all"

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Frontend (apps/web)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

[tasks."web:install"]
description = "Install apps/web dependencies with pnpm"
dir = "apps/web"
run = "pnpm install --frozen-lockfile || pnpm install"

[tasks."web:dev"]
description = "Vite dev server on :5173 proxying /api -> :3000"
dir = "apps/web"
run = "pnpm dev"

[tasks."web:build"]
description = "Build React app into apps/web/dist"
dir = "apps/web"
run = "pnpm build"

[tasks."web:lint"]
description = "Run ESLint on the frontend"
depends = ["web:install"]
dir = "apps/web"
run = "pnpm lint"

[tasks."web:typecheck"]
description = "Run the TS compiler without emitting"
depends = ["web:install"]
dir = "apps/web"
run = "pnpm typecheck"

[tasks."web:fmt"]
description = "Prettier write for frontend sources"
dir = "apps/web"
run = "pnpm format"

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Composite / meta tasks
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

[tasks.lint]
description = "Full lint: Rust fmt/clippy + frontend lint + typecheck"
depends = ["rust:lint", "web:lint", "web:typecheck"]

[tasks.fmt]
description = "Auto-format everything"
depends = ["rust:fmt", "web:fmt"]

[tasks.pre-commit]
description = "Fast checks for the git pre-commit hook"
depends = ["rust:lint"]

[tasks.ci]
description = "What CI runs on every pull request"
depends = ["lint", "test", "web:build", "cli:demo"]

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Running the app
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

[tasks."server:dev"]
description = "Axum server on 127.0.0.1:3000 serving embedded web/dist"
run = "cargo run -p orpheus-server -- --host 127.0.0.1 --port 3000"

[tasks.dev]
description = "Run axum (:3000) + Vite (:5173) together"
run = [
    { task = "server:dev" },
    { task = "web:dev" },
]

[tasks."cli:demo"]
description = "Run the bundled offline demo scan (synthetic fixtures)"
run = "cargo run -p orpheus-cli --locked -- demo"

[tasks."demo:fixtures"]
description = "Regenerate synthetic demo wallet fixtures"
run = "cargo run -p orpheus-demo-fixtures --locked"

[tasks."tauri:dev"]
description = "Tauri v2 desktop app in development mode"
dir = "crates/orpheus-tauri"
run = "pnpm tauri dev"

[tasks."tauri:build"]
description = "Package the Tauri desktop app for the current platform"
dir = "crates/orpheus-tauri"
run = "pnpm tauri build"

[tasks."release:setup-gpg"]
description = "One-time: generate the Orpheus release signing key + sync to GitHub secrets + .secrets"
# raw = true preserves the controlling TTY so the script's silent
# passphrase prompt (read -s) works.
raw = true
run = "./scripts/setup-gpg-release-key.sh"

content.text: Now the three CI workflows — `latest`, `release`, `nightly` — and `publish-crates`.

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Latest — continuous pre-release tracking `main`.
#
# On every push to main:
#   1. Build CLI + server binaries across native-runner matrix.
#   2. Build Tauri desktop bundles via tauri-action.
#   3. Atomically publish/replace the `latest` PRE-release on GitHub with
#      every artifact attached. The `latest` release is marked prerelease
#      so GitHub's "Latest release" pointer never tracks it — that pointer
#      is reserved for tagged releases created by release.yml.
#
# `${{ github.sha }}` etc. are bound into the job via env: blocks and then
# referenced as shell variables to neutralise any scripting-injection risk.

name: latest

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
  workflow_dispatch:

permissions:
  contents: read

concurrency:
  group: latest
  cancel-in-progress: true

env:
  CARGO_TERM_COLOR: always
  LATEST_TAG: latest

jobs:
  build-cli:
    name: cli / ${{ matrix.target }}
    runs-on: ${{ matrix.os }}
    strategy:
      fail-fast: false
      matrix:
        include:
          - target: x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu
            os: ubuntu-latest
            artifact: orpheus-linux-x86_64
            archive: tar.gz
          - target: aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu
            os: ubuntu-24.04-arm
            artifact: orpheus-linux-aarch64
            archive: tar.gz
          # Cross-compile x86_64 binaries from the Apple Silicon runner —
          # GitHub's macos-13 (Intel) runner pool is effectively unavailable
          # (jobs queue for hours and never start). `rustup target add` +
          # `cargo build --target x86_64-apple-darwin` on macos-latest
          # produces the same artifact.
          - target: x86_64-apple-darwin
            os: macos-latest
            artifact: orpheus-macos-x86_64
            archive: tar.gz
          - target: aarch64-apple-darwin
            os: macos-latest
            artifact: orpheus-macos-aarch64
            archive: tar.gz
          - target: x86_64-pc-windows-msvc
            os: windows-latest
            artifact: orpheus-windows-x86_64
            archive: zip
    env:
      TARGET: ${{ matrix.target }}
      ARTIFACT: ${{ matrix.artifact }}
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v6
      - uses: jdx/mise-action@v4
        with:
          experimental: true
          install: true
          cache: true
      - name: add rust target
        shell: bash
        run: rustup target add "$TARGET"
      - uses: Swatinem/rust-cache@v2
        with:
          shared-key: latest-cli-${{ matrix.target }}

      - name: build release binaries
        shell: bash
        run: cargo build --release --locked --target "$TARGET" -p orpheus-cli -p orpheus-server

      - name: stage artifact (unix)
        if: runner.os != 'Windows'
        shell: bash
        run: |
          mkdir -p "staging/$ARTIFACT"
          cp "target/$TARGET/release/orpheus" "staging/$ARTIFACT/"
          cp "target/$TARGET/release/orpheus-server" "staging/$ARTIFACT/"
          cp README.md LICENSE "staging/$ARTIFACT/"
          ( cd staging && tar -czf "$ARTIFACT.tar.gz" "$ARTIFACT" )

      - name: stage artifact (windows)
        if: runner.os == 'Windows'
        shell: pwsh
        run: |
          New-Item -ItemType Directory -Force -Path "staging/$env:ARTIFACT"
          Copy-Item "target/$env:TARGET/release/orpheus.exe" "staging/$env:ARTIFACT/"
          Copy-Item "target/$env:TARGET/release/orpheus-server.exe" "staging/$env:ARTIFACT/"
          Copy-Item README.md, LICENSE "staging/$env:ARTIFACT/"
          Compress-Archive -Path "staging/$env:ARTIFACT/*" -DestinationPath "staging/$env:ARTIFACT.zip"

      - uses: actions/upload-artifact@v7
        with:
          name: ${{ matrix.artifact }}
          path: staging/${{ matrix.artifact }}.${{ matrix.archive }}
          if-no-files-found: error

  build-tauri:
    name: tauri / ${{ matrix.name }}
    runs-on: ${{ matrix.os }}
    strategy:
      fail-fast: false
      matrix:
        include:
          - name: macos-universal
            os: macos-latest
            args: --target universal-apple-darwin
            rust-targets: aarch64-apple-darwin,x86_64-apple-darwin
          - name: linux-x86_64
            os: ubuntu-22.04
            args: ""
            rust-targets: ""
          - name: windows-x86_64
            os: windows-latest
            args: ""
            rust-targets: ""
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v6
      - uses: jdx/mise-action@v4
        with:
          experimental: true
          install: true
          cache: true
      - name: add rust targets
        if: matrix.rust-targets != ''
        shell: bash
        env:
          RUST_TARGETS: ${{ matrix.rust-targets }}
        run: |
          IFS=',' read -r -a targets <<< "$RUST_TARGETS"
          for t in "${targets[@]}"; do rustup target add "$t"; done
      - uses: Swatinem/rust-cache@v2
        with:
          workspaces: "crates/orpheus-tauri/src-tauri -> target"
          shared-key: latest-tauri-${{ matrix.name }}

      - uses: ./.github/actions/install-linux-deps

      - run: mise run web:install
      - uses: tauri-apps/tauri-action@v0
        env:
          GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
        with:
          projectPath: crates/orpheus-tauri/src-tauri
          args: ${{ matrix.args }}

      - name: collect tauri bundles
        shell: bash
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          mkdir -p tauri-output
          # Cargo workspace: bundles land under the workspace root `target/`.
          # Layout varies by target triple:
          #   native   -> target/release/bundle/<kind>/...
          #   crossed  -> target/<triple>/release/bundle/<kind>/...
          #               (e.g. target/universal-apple-darwin/release/bundle/...)
          # So search the whole target/ tree and filter by the
          # .../release/bundle/ path segment. Tauri v2 emits duplicates
          # (Orpheus_0.1.0_amd64.deb AND lowercase
          # orpheus_0.1.0_amd64.deb from the Cargo crate name); the
          # Orpheus_/Orpheus- name filter keeps only the productName-cased
          # ones.
          find target -type f -path "*/release/bundle/*" \
            \( -name "Orpheus_*.dmg"    -o -name "Orpheus*.app.tar.gz" \
               -o -name "Orpheus_*.AppImage" \
               -o -name "Orpheus_*.deb" -o -name "Orpheus-*.rpm" \
               -o -name "Orpheus_*.msi" -o -name "Orpheus_*.exe" \) \
            -exec cp {} tauri-output/ \;
          ls -la tauri-output/
          if [ -z "$(ls -A tauri-output/)" ]; then
            echo "::error::no Tauri bundles found under target/**/release/bundle"
            find target -type d -name bundle 2>&1 | head -20 || true
            exit 1
          fi

      - uses: actions/upload-artifact@v7
        with:
          name: tauri-${{ matrix.name }}
          path: tauri-output/*
          if-no-files-found: error

  publish-latest:
    name: publish latest pre-release
    needs: [build-cli, build-tauri]
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      contents: write
    env:
      SHA: ${{ github.sha }}
      RUN_ID: ${{ github.run_id }}
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v6
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0

      - uses: actions/download-artifact@v8
        with:
          path: dist
          merge-multiple: true

      - name: list artifacts
        run: ls -la dist/

      - name: sign artifacts with gpg
        uses: ./.github/actions/sign-artifacts
        with:
          artifacts-dir: dist
          gpg-private-key: ${{ secrets.GPG_PRIVATE_KEY }}
          gpg-passphrase: ${{ secrets.GPG_PASSPHRASE }}
          gpg-key-id: ${{ secrets.GPG_KEY_ID }}

      - name: delete previous latest release
        env:
          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
        run: gh release delete "$LATEST_TAG" --yes --cleanup-tag || true

      - name: create latest pre-release
        uses: softprops/action-gh-release@v3
        with:
          tag_name: ${{ env.LATEST_TAG }}
          name: "Latest (main @ ${{ github.sha }})"
          prerelease: true
          make_latest: "false"
          body: |
            **Rolling pre-release built from `main`.** Do not depend on it for
            reproducible builds — it changes on every push. For a stable
            version, use a tagged release.

            - commit: `${{ github.sha }}`
            - run:    `${{ github.run_id }}`

            All artifacts are GPG-signed. Verify with:
            ```
            gpg --verify SHA256SUMS.asc SHA256SUMS
            sha256sum -c SHA256SUMS
            ```
          files: dist/*
          fail_on_unmatched_files: true

  publish-cask-head:
    name: publish head cask
    needs: [publish-latest]
    uses: ./.github/workflows/publish-cask-head.yml
    secrets: inherit

content.text:
Pausing the refactor to answer cleanly.

**Short answer: yes — and it'd pair naturally with the refactor we're doing.**

Today's cask templates ([orpheus.rb.universal.template:19](packaging/homebrew/orpheus.rb.universal.template#L19), etc.) only install the `.app`. The `orpheus` CLI binary ships as a separate tarball on the same release but isn't symlinked. Three ways to make one `brew install` deliver both:

1. **CLI symlinked out of the `.app` (recommended).** Have Tauri bundle the `orpheus` binary inside `Orpheus.app/Contents/Resources/bin/orpheus`, then add one line to the cask:
   ```ruby
   binary "#{appdir}/Orpheus.app/Contents/Resources/bin/orpheus"
   ```
   Homebrew symlinks it to `/opt/homebrew/bin/orpheus`. One download, one install, both artifacts versioned together. No separate URL in the cask.

2. **Cask + companion formula.** A second Ruby file `Formula/orpheus.rb` wraps the tarball; the cask declares `depends_on formula: "orpheus"`. Two files to maintain and one extra download at install time, but totally standard Homebrew pattern.

3. **Second `url` with `preflight`.** Cask manually curls the CLI tarball and places `orpheus` in `bin/` via a `preflight` hook. Works, but it's the most bespoke of the three and hides the download from Homebrew's cache.

**Recommendation: option 1.** Small change in `crates/orpheus-tauri/src-tauri/tauri.conf.json` (add `resources` entry + a pre-build step that copies `target/$TARGET/release/orpheus` into the bundle) and one `binary` line in each cask template. Works identically on `-head` and stable. Same approach for both Intel/ARM DMGs and the universal one.

Want me to add this to the current PR, or keep this PR focused on the server→CLI merge and do the cask piece in a follow-up?
yes
Resuming the refactor, then adding the cask+Tauri bundling piece. Finishing CI edits next.

toolUseResult.oldString:
# Order matters: orpheus-core must publish before orpheus-cli and
# orpheus-server because those depend on it via path + version. Cargo
# usually discovers this via `cargo publish --workspace`, but we publish
# explicitly for predictability and better error reporting.
toolUseResult.newString:
# Order matters: orpheus-core must publish before orpheus-cli because the
# CLI depends on it via path + version. Cargo usually discovers this via
# `cargo publish --workspace`, but we publish explicitly for predictability
# and better error reporting.
toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Publish crates to crates.io — called by release.yml after a tagged
# release is live on GitHub.
#
# Only publishes library + binary crates that have `publish = true`
# (the default). `orpheus-tauri` and `orpheus-demo-fixtures` are marked
# `publish = false` and skipped automatically by cargo.
#
# Order matters: orpheus-core must publish before orpheus-cli and
# orpheus-server because those depend on it via path + version. Cargo
# usually discovers this via `cargo publish --workspace`, but we publish
# explicitly for predictability and better error reporting.

name: publish-crates

on:
  workflow_call:
    inputs:
      tag:
        description: "Release tag (e.g. v0.1.1). The crate version must match tag-less."
        required: true
        type: string
  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      tag:
        description: "Release tag (e.g. v0.1.1)."
        required: true
        type: string
      dry_run:
        description: "If true, run cargo publish --dry-run only."
        required: false
        type: boolean
        default: false

permissions:
  contents: read

concurrency:
  group: publish-crates-${{ inputs.tag }}
  cancel-in-progress: false

jobs:
  publish:
    name: cargo publish
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    env:
      TAG: ${{ inputs.tag }}
      DRY_RUN: ${{ inputs.dry_run || 'false' }}
      CARGO_REGISTRY_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.CARGO_REGISTRY_TOKEN }}
    steps:
      - name: check CARGO_REGISTRY_TOKEN is set
        id: gate
        env:
          TOKEN: ${{ secrets.CARGO_REGISTRY_TOKEN }}
        shell: bash
        run: |
          if [[ -z "$TOKEN" ]]; then
            echo "::warning::CARGO_REGISTRY_TOKEN not set; skipping crates.io publish"
            echo "skip=true" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          else
            echo "skip=false" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          fi

      - if: steps.gate.outputs.skip == 'false'
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          ref: ${{ inputs.tag }}
          fetch-depth: 0

      - if: steps.gate.outputs.skip == 'false'
        uses: jdx/mise-action@v2
        with:
          experimental: true
          install: true
          cache: true

      - if: steps.gate.outputs.skip == 'false'
        uses: Swatinem/rust-cache@v2
        with:
          shared-key: publish-crates

      - if: steps.gate.outputs.skip == 'false'
        name: verify crate versions match the tag
        shell: bash
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          EXPECTED="${TAG#v}"
          WORKSPACE_VERSION=$(grep -E '^version\s*=' Cargo.toml | head -1 | sed -E 's/.*"([^"]+)".*/\1/')
          echo "tag: $TAG  -> expected crate version: $EXPECTED"
          echo "workspace version: $WORKSPACE_VERSION"
          if [[ "$EXPECTED" != "$WORKSPACE_VERSION" ]]; then
            echo "::error::tag ($EXPECTED) does not match workspace.version ($WORKSPACE_VERSION)"
            exit 1
          fi

      - name: cargo publish (dry-run)
        if: steps.gate.outputs.skip == 'false' && env.DRY_RUN == 'true'
        shell: bash
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          for c in orpheus-core orpheus-cli orpheus-server; do
            echo "=== dry-run: $c ==="
            cargo publish --dry-run -p "$c"
          done

      - name: cargo publish (real)
        if: steps.gate.outputs.skip == 'false' && env.DRY_RUN != 'true'
        shell: bash
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          # Publish in dependency order. Between crates, pause briefly to let
          # the crates.io index catch up so dependents can resolve the new
          # version. `--no-verify` on dependents avoids a redundant full build.
          publish_one() {
            local crate=$1
            shift
            echo "=== publishing: $crate ==="
            # Allow failure only for the specific "already uploaded" case so
            # re-runs are idempotent.
            if ! cargo publish -p "$crate" "$@"; then
              if cargo search "$crate" | grep -q "^$crate = \"${TAG#v}\""; then
                echo "$crate@${TAG#v} already on crates.io; skipping"
                return 0
              fi
              return 1
            fi
          }
          publish_one orpheus-core
          # Give crates.io a moment to index the new version.
          sleep 30
          publish_one orpheus-cli --no-verify
          publish_one orpheus-server --no-verify

      - name: cargo publish (real)
        if: steps.gate.outputs.skip == 'false' && env.DRY_RUN != 'true'
        shell: bash
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          # Publish in dependency order. Between crates, pause briefly to let
          # the crates.io index catch up so dependents can resolve the new
          # version. `--no-verify` on dependents avoids a redundant full build.
          publish_one() {
            local crate=$1
            shift
            echo "=== publishing: $crate ==="
            # Allow failure only for the specific "already uploaded" case so
            # re-runs are idempotent.
            if ! cargo publish -p "$crate" "$@"; then
              if cargo search "$crate" | grep -q "^$crate = \"${TAG#v}\""; then
                echo "$crate@${TAG#v} already on crates.io; skipping"
                return 0
              fi
              return 1
            fi
          }
          publish_one orpheus-core
          # Give crates.io a moment to index the new version.
          sleep 30
          publish_one orpheus-cli --no-verify
toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Publish crates to crates.io — called by release.yml after a tagged
# release is live on GitHub.
#
# Only publishes library + binary crates that have `publish = true`
# (the default). `orpheus-tauri` and `orpheus-demo-fixtures` are marked
# `publish = false` and skipped automatically by cargo.
#
# Order matters: orpheus-core must publish before orpheus-cli because the
# CLI depends on it via path + version. Cargo usually discovers this via
# `cargo publish --workspace`, but we publish explicitly for predictability
# and better error reporting.

name: publish-crates

on:
  workflow_call:
    inputs:
      tag:
        description: "Release tag (e.g. v0.1.1). The crate version must match tag-less."
        required: true
        type: string
  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      tag:
        description: "Release tag (e.g. v0.1.1)."
        required: true
        type: string
      dry_run:
        description: "If true, run cargo publish --dry-run only."
        required: false
        type: boolean
        default: false

permissions:
  contents: read

concurrency:
  group: publish-crates-${{ inputs.tag }}
  cancel-in-progress: false

jobs:
  publish:
    name: cargo publish
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    env:
      TAG: ${{ inputs.tag }}
      DRY_RUN: ${{ inputs.dry_run || 'false' }}
      CARGO_REGISTRY_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.CARGO_REGISTRY_TOKEN }}
    steps:
      - name: check CARGO_REGISTRY_TOKEN is set
        id: gate
        env:
          TOKEN: ${{ secrets.CARGO_REGISTRY_TOKEN }}
        shell: bash
        run: |
          if [[ -z "$TOKEN" ]]; then
            echo "::warning::CARGO_REGISTRY_TOKEN not set; skipping crates.io publish"
            echo "skip=true" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          else
            echo "skip=false" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          fi

      - if: steps.gate.outputs.skip == 'false'
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          ref: ${{ inputs.tag }}
          fetch-depth: 0

      - if: steps.gate.outputs.skip == 'false'
        uses: jdx/mise-action@v2
        with:
          experimental: true
          install: true
          cache: true

      - if: steps.gate.outputs.skip == 'false'
        uses: Swatinem/rust-cache@v2
        with:
          shared-key: publish-crates

      - if: steps.gate.outputs.skip == 'false'
        name: verify crate versions match the tag
        shell: bash
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          EXPECTED="${TAG#v}"
          WORKSPACE_VERSION=$(grep -E '^version\s*=' Cargo.toml | head -1 | sed -E 's/.*"([^"]+)".*/\1/')
          echo "tag: $TAG  -> expected crate version: $EXPECTED"
          echo "workspace version: $WORKSPACE_VERSION"
          if [[ "$EXPECTED" != "$WORKSPACE_VERSION" ]]; then
            echo "::error::tag ($EXPECTED) does not match workspace.version ($WORKSPACE_VERSION)"
            exit 1
          fi

      - name: cargo publish (dry-run)
        if: steps.gate.outputs.skip == 'false' && env.DRY_RUN == 'true'
        shell: bash
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          for c in orpheus-core orpheus-cli orpheus-server; do
            echo "=== dry-run: $c ==="
            cargo publish --dry-run -p "$c"
          done

      - name: cargo publish (real)
        if: steps.gate.outputs.skip == 'false' && env.DRY_RUN != 'true'
        shell: bash
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          # Publish in dependency order. Between crates, pause briefly to let
          # the crates.io index catch up so dependents can resolve the new
          # version. `--no-verify` on dependents avoids a redundant full build.
          publish_one() {
            local crate=$1
            shift
            echo "=== publishing: $crate ==="
            # Allow failure only for the specific "already uploaded" case so
            # re-runs are idempotent.
            if ! cargo publish -p "$crate" "$@"; then
              if cargo search "$crate" | grep -q "^$crate = \"${TAG#v}\""; then
                echo "$crate@${TAG#v} already on crates.io; skipping"
                return 0
              fi
              return 1
            fi
          }
          publish_one orpheus-core
          # Give crates.io a moment to index the new version.
          sleep 30
          publish_one orpheus-cli --no-verify
          publish_one orpheus-server --no-verify

+          for c in orpheus-core orpheus-cli; do
# Give crates.io a moment to index the new version.
