toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Orpheus — developer environment managed by mise (https://mise.jdx.dev).
#
# mise is the single source of truth for tool versions and for every
# scripted task. The same `mise run <task>` commands are invoked from
# local shells, git hooks, and CI (see .github/workflows/*.yml).
#
# On `cd` into the repo (with mise's shell integration active) the pinned
# toolchain versions auto-activate. Run `mise install` to fetch them the
# first time.

[tools]
rust = "1.95"
node = "22"
pnpm = "9"
# Tauri build prerequisites on macOS are Xcode Command Line Tools; on Linux
# users need libwebkit2gtk-4.1-dev + friends (see .github/workflows/ci.yml).

[env]
# rustup-managed toolchain always wins over /opt/homebrew/bin/{cargo,rustc}.
# Windows CI runners don't set $HOME — fall back to %USERPROFILE% there so
# mise can still render the template.
_.path = [
    "{% if env.HOME %}{{ env.HOME }}/.cargo/bin{% elif env.USERPROFILE %}{{ env.USERPROFILE }}/.cargo/bin{% else %}{{ config_root }}/.cargo/bin{% endif %}",
]
RUST_BACKTRACE = "1"
RUST_LOG       = "orpheus=info,tower_http=info"

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Lifecycle hooks
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# "enter" fires once per shell when mise activates this project. We use it
# to install git hooks on the first entry of a fresh clone.
[hooks]
enter = "test -x .git/hooks/pre-commit || mise run hooks:install"

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Setup / housekeeping
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

[tasks.setup]
description = "Install all Rust + frontend dependencies"
run = [
    "cargo fetch --locked || cargo fetch",
    "mise run web:install",
]

[tasks."hooks:install"]
description = "Symlink git hooks from .githooks/ into .git/hooks/"
run = """
set -e
mkdir -p .git/hooks
for hook in .githooks/*; do
    name=$(basename "$hook")
    ln -sf "../../$hook" ".git/hooks/$name"
done
echo "git hooks installed from .githooks/"
"""

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Rust
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

[tasks.build]
description = "Build the whole Rust workspace (debug)"
run = "cargo build --workspace --all-targets"

[tasks."build:release"]
description = "Release build of the unified `orpheus` binary into target/release"
run = "cargo build --release --locked -p orpheus-cli"

[tasks.install]
description = "Install `orpheus` into ~/.cargo/bin"
run = "cargo install --path crates/orpheus-cli --locked --force"

[tasks.test]
description = "Run all Rust tests"
# orpheus-tauri has no tests of its own. clippy --workspace compile-checks
# it and latest.yml handles the full tauri build for releases.
run = "cargo test --workspace --locked --exclude orpheus-tauri"

[tasks."test:core"]
description = "Run orpheus-core tests only"
run = "cargo test -p orpheus-core --locked"

[tasks."rust:lint"]
description = "Cargo fmt --check + clippy"
run = [
    "cargo fmt --all -- --check",
    "cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings",
]

[tasks."rust:fmt"]
description = "Auto-format Rust sources"
run = "cargo fmt --all"

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Frontend (apps/web)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

[tasks."web:install"]
description = "Install apps/web dependencies with pnpm"
dir = "apps/web"
run = "pnpm install --frozen-lockfile || pnpm install"

[tasks."web:dev"]
description = "Vite dev server on :5173 proxying /api -> :3000"
dir = "apps/web"
run = "pnpm dev"

[tasks."web:build"]
description = "Build React app into apps/web/dist"
dir = "apps/web"
run = "pnpm build"

[tasks."web:lint"]
description = "Run ESLint on the frontend"
depends = ["web:install"]
dir = "apps/web"
run = "pnpm lint"

[tasks."web:typecheck"]
description = "Run the TS compiler without emitting"
depends = ["web:install"]
dir = "apps/web"
run = "pnpm typecheck"

[tasks."web:fmt"]
description = "Prettier write for frontend sources"
dir = "apps/web"
run = "pnpm format"

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Composite / meta tasks
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

[tasks.lint]
description = "Full lint: Rust fmt/clippy + frontend lint + typecheck"
depends = ["rust:lint", "web:lint", "web:typecheck"]

[tasks.fmt]
description = "Auto-format everything"
depends = ["rust:fmt", "web:fmt"]

[tasks.pre-commit]
description = "Fast checks for the git pre-commit hook"
depends = ["rust:lint"]

[tasks.ci]
description = "What CI runs on every pull request"
depends = ["lint", "test", "web:build", "cli:demo"]

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Running the app
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

[tasks."server:dev"]
description = "`orpheus serve` on 127.0.0.1:3000 serving embedded web/dist + /api"
run = "cargo run -p orpheus-cli -- serve --host 127.0.0.1 --port 3000"

[tasks.dev]
description = "Run axum (:3000) + Vite (:5173) together"
run = [
    { task = "server:dev" },
    { task = "web:dev" },
]

[tasks."cli:demo"]
description = "Run the bundled offline demo scan (synthetic fixtures)"
run = "cargo run -p orpheus-cli --locked -- demo"

[tasks."demo:fixtures"]
description = "Regenerate synthetic demo wallet fixtures"
run = "cargo run -p orpheus-demo-fixtures --locked"

[tasks."tauri:dev"]
description = "Tauri v2 desktop app in development mode"
dir = "crates/orpheus-tauri"
run = "pnpm tauri dev"

[tasks."tauri:build"]
description = "Package the Tauri desktop app for the current platform"
dir = "crates/orpheus-tauri"
run = "pnpm tauri build"

[tasks."release:setup-gpg"]
description = "One-time: generate the Orpheus release signing key + sync to GitHub secrets + .secrets"
# raw = true preserves the controlling TTY so the script's silent
# passphrase prompt (read -s) works.
raw = true
run = "./scripts/setup-gpg-release-key.sh"

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Latest — continuous pre-release tracking `main`.
#
# On every push to main:
#   1. Build CLI + server binaries across native-runner matrix.
#   2. Build Tauri desktop bundles via tauri-action.
#   3. Atomically publish/replace the `latest` PRE-release on GitHub with
#      every artifact attached. The `latest` release is marked prerelease
#      so GitHub's "Latest release" pointer never tracks it — that pointer
#      is reserved for tagged releases created by release.yml.
#
# `${{ github.sha }}` etc. are bound into the job via env: blocks and then
# referenced as shell variables to neutralise any scripting-injection risk.

name: latest

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
  workflow_dispatch:

permissions:
  contents: read

concurrency:
  group: latest
  cancel-in-progress: true

env:
  CARGO_TERM_COLOR: always
  LATEST_TAG: latest

jobs:
  build-cli:
    name: cli / ${{ matrix.target }}
    runs-on: ${{ matrix.os }}
    strategy:
      fail-fast: false
      matrix:
        include:
          - target: x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu
            os: ubuntu-latest
            artifact: orpheus-linux-x86_64
            archive: tar.gz
          - target: aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu
            os: ubuntu-24.04-arm
            artifact: orpheus-linux-aarch64
            archive: tar.gz
          # Cross-compile x86_64 binaries from the Apple Silicon runner —
          # GitHub's macos-13 (Intel) runner pool is effectively unavailable
          # (jobs queue for hours and never start). `rustup target add` +
          # `cargo build --target x86_64-apple-darwin` on macos-latest
          # produces the same artifact.
          - target: x86_64-apple-darwin
            os: macos-latest
            artifact: orpheus-macos-x86_64
            archive: tar.gz
          - target: aarch64-apple-darwin
            os: macos-latest
            artifact: orpheus-macos-aarch64
            archive: tar.gz
          - target: x86_64-pc-windows-msvc
            os: windows-latest
            artifact: orpheus-windows-x86_64
            archive: zip
    env:
      TARGET: ${{ matrix.target }}
      ARTIFACT: ${{ matrix.artifact }}
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v6
      - uses: jdx/mise-action@v4
        with:
          experimental: true
          install: true
          cache: true
      - name: add rust target
        shell: bash
        run: rustup target add "$TARGET"
      - uses: Swatinem/rust-cache@v2
        with:
          shared-key: latest-cli-${{ matrix.target }}

      - name: build release binary
        shell: bash
        run: cargo build --release --locked --target "$TARGET" -p orpheus-cli

      - name: stage artifact (unix)
        if: runner.os != 'Windows'
        shell: bash
        run: |
          mkdir -p "staging/$ARTIFACT"
          cp "target/$TARGET/release/orpheus" "staging/$ARTIFACT/"
          cp README.md LICENSE "staging/$ARTIFACT/"
          ( cd staging && tar -czf "$ARTIFACT.tar.gz" "$ARTIFACT" )

      - name: stage artifact (windows)
        if: runner.os == 'Windows'
        shell: pwsh
        run: |
          New-Item -ItemType Directory -Force -Path "staging/$env:ARTIFACT"
          Copy-Item "target/$env:TARGET/release/orpheus.exe" "staging/$env:ARTIFACT/"
          Copy-Item README.md, LICENSE "staging/$env:ARTIFACT/"
          Compress-Archive -Path "staging/$env:ARTIFACT/*" -DestinationPath "staging/$env:ARTIFACT.zip"

      - uses: actions/upload-artifact@v7
        with:
          name: ${{ matrix.artifact }}
          path: staging/${{ matrix.artifact }}.${{ matrix.archive }}
          if-no-files-found: error

  build-tauri:
    name: tauri / ${{ matrix.name }}
    runs-on: ${{ matrix.os }}
    strategy:
      fail-fast: false
      matrix:
        include:
          - name: macos-universal
            os: macos-latest
            args: --target universal-apple-darwin
            rust-targets: aarch64-apple-darwin,x86_64-apple-darwin
          - name: linux-x86_64
            os: ubuntu-22.04
            args: ""
            rust-targets: ""
          - name: windows-x86_64
            os: windows-latest
            args: ""
            rust-targets: ""
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v6
      - uses: jdx/mise-action@v4
        with:
          experimental: true
          install: true
          cache: true
      - name: add rust targets
        if: matrix.rust-targets != ''
        shell: bash
        env:
          RUST_TARGETS: ${{ matrix.rust-targets }}
        run: |
          IFS=',' read -r -a targets <<< "$RUST_TARGETS"
          for t in "${targets[@]}"; do rustup target add "$t"; done
      - uses: Swatinem/rust-cache@v2
        with:
          workspaces: "crates/orpheus-tauri/src-tauri -> target"
          shared-key: latest-tauri-${{ matrix.name }}

      - uses: ./.github/actions/install-linux-deps

      - run: mise run web:install
      - uses: tauri-apps/tauri-action@v0
        env:
          GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
        with:
          projectPath: crates/orpheus-tauri/src-tauri
          args: ${{ matrix.args }}

      - name: collect tauri bundles
        shell: bash
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          mkdir -p tauri-output
          # Cargo workspace: bundles land under the workspace root `target/`.
          # Layout varies by target triple:
          #   native   -> target/release/bundle/<kind>/...
          #   crossed  -> target/<triple>/release/bundle/<kind>/...
          #               (e.g. target/universal-apple-darwin/release/bundle/...)
          # So search the whole target/ tree and filter by the
          # .../release/bundle/ path segment. Tauri v2 emits duplicates
          # (Orpheus_0.1.0_amd64.deb AND lowercase
          # orpheus_0.1.0_amd64.deb from the Cargo crate name); the
          # Orpheus_/Orpheus- name filter keeps only the productName-cased
          # ones.
          find target -type f -path "*/release/bundle/*" \
            \( -name "Orpheus_*.dmg"    -o -name "Orpheus*.app.tar.gz" \
               -o -name "Orpheus_*.AppImage" \
               -o -name "Orpheus_*.deb" -o -name "Orpheus-*.rpm" \
               -o -name "Orpheus_*.msi" -o -name "Orpheus_*.exe" \) \
            -exec cp {} tauri-output/ \;
          ls -la tauri-output/
          if [ -z "$(ls -A tauri-output/)" ]; then
            echo "::error::no Tauri bundles found under target/**/release/bundle"
            find target -type d -name bundle 2>&1 | head -20 || true
            exit 1
          fi

      - uses: actions/upload-artifact@v7
        with:
          name: tauri-${{ matrix.name }}
          path: tauri-output/*
          if-no-files-found: error

  publish-latest:
    name: publish latest pre-release
    needs: [build-cli, build-tauri]
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      contents: write
    env:
      SHA: ${{ github.sha }}
      RUN_ID: ${{ github.run_id }}
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v6
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0

      - uses: actions/download-artifact@v8
        with:
          path: dist
          merge-multiple: true

      - name: list artifacts
        run: ls -la dist/

      - name: sign artifacts with gpg
        uses: ./.github/actions/sign-artifacts
        with:
          artifacts-dir: dist
          gpg-private-key: ${{ secrets.GPG_PRIVATE_KEY }}
          gpg-passphrase: ${{ secrets.GPG_PASSPHRASE }}
          gpg-key-id: ${{ secrets.GPG_KEY_ID }}

      - name: delete previous latest release
        env:
          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
        run: gh release delete "$LATEST_TAG" --yes --cleanup-tag || true

      - name: create latest pre-release
        uses: softprops/action-gh-release@v3
        with:
          tag_name: ${{ env.LATEST_TAG }}
          name: "Latest (main @ ${{ github.sha }})"
          prerelease: true
          make_latest: "false"
          body: |
            **Rolling pre-release built from `main`.** Do not depend on it for
            reproducible builds — it changes on every push. For a stable
            version, use a tagged release.

            - commit: `${{ github.sha }}`
            - run:    `${{ github.run_id }}`

            All artifacts are GPG-signed. Verify with:
            ```
            gpg --verify SHA256SUMS.asc SHA256SUMS
            sha256sum -c SHA256SUMS
            ```
          files: dist/*
          fail_on_unmatched_files: true

  publish-cask-head:
    name: publish head cask
    needs: [publish-latest]
    uses: ./.github/workflows/publish-cask-head.yml
    secrets: inherit

content.text: Now add the `binary` stanza to the three cask templates.

input.old_string:
auto_updates false
  depends_on macos: ">= :monterey"

  app "Orpheus.app"

  caveats <<~EOS
input.new_string:
auto_updates false
  depends_on macos: ">= :monterey"

  app "Orpheus.app"
  # `orpheus` CLI is bundled inside Orpheus.app via Tauri externalBin.

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Orpheus

![Orpheus](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/33de7189-3eaf-4239-b696-e03cac7c078a)

> **καταβαίνω** — *to descend*
>
> Recover lost cryptocurrency from forgotten wallets.

Orpheus is a Rust + Tauri v2 tool for extracting private keys from old Bitcoin
wallet files (Bitcoin Core `wallet.dat`, MultiBit Classic `.wallet`, Bitcoin
Core `dumpwallet` output, BIP39 seed phrases, and blockchain.com legacy
mnemonics). One `orpheus` binary delivers the CLI, an embedded React UI via
`orpheus serve`, and — paired with the Tauri shell — a native desktop app,
all sharing the same extraction core.

## Architecture

```text
orpheus/
├── Cargo.toml              # workspace
├── mise.toml               # toolchain + tasks + hooks
├── rust-toolchain.toml
├── crates/
│   ├── orpheus-core/       # extractors, crypto, balance, scanner
│   ├── orpheus-cli/        # clap CLI: scan / extract / mnemonic / demo / serve
│   │                       # `serve` bundles axum + rust-embed for apps/web/dist + /api
│   ├── orpheus-tauri/      # Tauri v2 desktop shell; same API, no sidecar
│   └── orpheus-demo-fixtures/
└── apps/
    └── web/                # Vite + React 19 + TS + Tailwind v4 + shadcn
```

The `orpheus-core` crate is the single source of truth. The `orpheus`
CLI and the Tauri shell both delegate to it.

## Getting started

Orpheus is managed by **[mise](https://mise.jdx.dev)**. It's the single
source of truth for toolchain versions (see `[tools]` in `mise.toml`) and
for every scripted task — local development and CI run the same
`mise run <task>` commands.

```bash
# Install mise once: https://mise.jdx.dev
mise install              # fetch rust 1.95, node 22, pnpm 9
mise run setup            # cargo fetch + pnpm install
```

On `cd` into the repo mise auto-activates the toolchain and installs the
git pre-commit hook (`.githooks/pre-commit` → `mise run pre-commit` =
`cargo fmt --check` + `cargo clippy -D warnings`).

### Common tasks

```bash
mise run build           # cargo build --workspace
mise run test            # cargo test --workspace
mise run lint            # fmt --check + clippy + pnpm lint
mise run fmt             # cargo fmt + prettier
mise run cli:demo        # offline demo via CLI
mise run server:dev      # `orpheus serve` on 127.0.0.1:3000
mise run web:dev         # Vite dev server on :5173 proxying /api → :3000
mise run dev             # both above together
mise run tauri:dev       # desktop app in development mode
mise run tauri:build     # package desktop app
```

## Try the offline demo

No wallets, no network. Runs against synthesized fixtures:

```bash
cargo run -p orpheus-demo-fixtures  # regenerate fixtures/demo-wallets/
cargo run -p orpheus-cli -- demo
```

Output: 5 wallets, 325 keys, a synthetic 0.08730104 BTC including a
homage to the 0.03865052 BTC recovered in the original session that
seeded this project.

## Supported wallet formats

| Format                                    | Extractor             |
|-------------------------------------------|-----------------------|
| Bitcoin Core `wallet.dat` (BDB or SQLite) | `bitcoin_core`        |
| MultiBit Classic `.wallet` (unencrypted)  | `multibit`            |
| MultiBit Classic `.wallet` (v3 scrypt+AES)| `encrypted`           |
| Bitcoin Core `dumpwallet` text            | `wallet_dump`         |
| Bitcoin Core `listdescriptors` JSON       | `wallet_dump`         |
| BIP39 mnemonic (12/15/18/21/24 words)     | `bip39_mnemonic`      |
| blockchain.com legacy 1626/65591 mnemonic | `blockchain_com`      |

BIP39 derivation runs BIP44, BIP49, BIP84, *and* Breadwallet's legacy
`m/0'/{0,1}/x` — a first-class path anchored by a regression test for
the 2013-era iOS wallet that motivated this project.

## Balance providers

Every recovered address can be enriched with balance + received-total +
tx-count via `--provider`:

| Value         | Source                                                        |
|---------------|---------------------------------------------------------------|
| `blockstream` | https://blockstream.info/api (public esplora, no API key) — **default** |
| `blockchain`  | https://blockchain.info/balance (public, batched up to 20 addrs) |
| `mock`        | offline lookup against `--mock-file` JSON (used by `orpheus demo`) |
| `none`        | skip balance lookup entirely                                  |

The same identifiers work in `--provider <name>` on the CLI, the
`ORPHEUS_PROVIDER` env var, and the `Balance provider` dropdown in the
web UI. Use `none` or `mock` on air-gapped machines; the default
deliberately calls out to the internet so first-run scans report real
balances without extra flags.

## Releases & versioning

This repo follows [Conventional Commits](https://www.conventionalcommits.org/).
Release notes for every tagged release are generated from the commit
range and grouped by type (Features / Bug Fixes / Performance / …). See
[CONTRIBUTING.md](CONTRIBUTING.md#commit-messages--conventional-commits)
for the conventions we enforce.

**Where to grab builds:**

- [**Latest release**](https://github.com/dougnukem/orpheus/releases/latest) —
  the most recent signed, tagged release. Use this.
- [**`latest` pre-release**](https://github.com/dougnukem/orpheus/releases/tag/latest) —
  rolling build of `main`; useful for bleeding-edge smoke-testing.

Artifacts published for every tagged release:

- `orpheus-vX.Y.Z-{linux,macos,windows}-{x86_64,aarch64}.{tar.gz,zip}` —
  standalone `orpheus` binary (CLI commands + `orpheus serve`)
- Tauri desktop bundles (`.dmg` universal macOS, `.AppImage` + `.deb` Linux,
  `.msi` Windows)
- `SHA256SUMS` for integrity verification

## Security

- **Never feed real wallets to an untrusted build.** Audit the code first
  or pin a commit you've reviewed.
- **Offline by default.** The balance provider defaults to `mock` (fully
  offline); only opt-in to blockchain.info / blockstream.info.
- **Ephemeral Docker mode** is recommended for high-value recovery —
  mount the wallet directory read-only, run with `--network none` when
  not fetching balances, shut down when done.
- **Clear your scrollback** after a successful extraction. WIF keys in
  your terminal history are keys in your terminal history.

## License

MIT. See `LICENSE`.

+  delivers both the app *and* an `orpheus` on `$PATH`.

lines: +//! so the packaged desktop app ships the unified `orpheus` CLI alongside
+//! that the expected per-triple file exists at compile time — which would
+//! break plain `cargo check`/`cargo build` in a fresh clone that hasn't
+//! To keep workspace-level builds ergonomic we write an empty placeholder
+//! here if none exists. `mise run tauri:cli` (auto-invoked by `tauri:dev`
+//! / `tauri:build`) and the `build-tauri` CI jobs overwrite it with the
+//! real binary before Tauri bundles the `.app` / `.dmg` / `.AppImage` /
+//! `.msi`, so release artifacts always ship the genuine article.
+    let target = env::var("TARGET").expect("TARGET set by cargo");

should run `orpheus serve` and downstream cargo/binstall/crates.io
consumers should depend on `orpheus-cli`.

EOF
)"

input.command:
gh pr create --draft --title "refactor(cli)!: merge orpheus-server into orpheus CLI and bundle CLI in Tauri app" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## Summary

- **Single binary.** Collapses the `orpheus-server` crate into a new `orpheus serve` subcommand on the existing `orpheus` CLI. One build artifact now delivers scan/extract/mnemonic/demo *and* the axum JSON API with the embedded React UI from `apps/web/dist`.
- **`brew install --cask orpheus` now installs the CLI too.** The Tauri desktop bundle embeds the `orpheus` binary via `bundle.externalBin`, and the three cask templates gain a `binary` stanza pointing at it. macOS users get the `.app` *and* `orpheus` on `\$PATH` in a single install. Linux `.deb`/`.rpm`/AppImage bundles also carry the CLI.
- **No behavioural change.** `orpheus serve --host --port --mock-file --demo-dir` takes the same flags the old `orpheus-server` did; `mise run server:dev` just shells out to it. Smoke-tested locally.

## What moved

| Before | After |
|---|---|
| `crates/orpheus-server/src/main.rs` | `crates/orpheus-cli/src/serve.rs` |
| `crates/orpheus-server/build.rs` | `crates/orpheus-cli/build.rs` |
| `cargo run -p orpheus-server -- --host …` | `cargo run -p orpheus-cli -- serve --host …` |
| `cargo publish orpheus-server` | removed from `publish-crates.yml` |
| cask installs `.app` only | cask also symlinks the bundled `orpheus` binary to `/opt/homebrew/bin/orpheus` |

## CI impact

- `latest.yml` / `release.yml` / `nightly.yml` matrices drop `-p orpheus-server` and the corresponding `cp orpheus-server` staging steps.
- `build-tauri` jobs get a new **stage orpheus CLI for tauri externalBin** step that calls `scripts/build-cli-for-tauri.sh` before `tauri-apps/tauri-action@v0`. Per-triple for native builds, both aarch64+x86_64 for macOS universal.
- `publish-crates.yml` now publishes only `orpheus-core` and `orpheus-cli`.
- `orpheus-tauri`'s `build.rs` writes an empty placeholder at `binaries/orpheus-<triple>[.exe]` so `cargo check --workspace` stays green in fresh clones — `mise run tauri:cli` (auto-invoked by `tauri:dev`/`tauri:build`) and CI overwrite it with the real binary before the bundle step runs.

## Breaking change

- `orpheus-server` crate + binary are gone from the workspace and crates.io.
- Users should migrate to `orpheus serve`.

## Test plan

- [x] `mise run ci` locally (rust fmt/clippy, web lint/typecheck, tests, cli:demo) — all green
- [x] `orpheus serve --help` renders new subcommand with existing flags
- [x] `mise run tauri:cli` stages a 6.8 MB mach-o arm64 binary at the expected path
- [x] `cargo check --workspace` on a fresh clone (simulated) — placeholder build.rs keeps it compiling
- [ ] CI green across all platforms (will verify on push)
- [ ] `mise run tauri:build` produces a `.dmg` that, when installed, exposes `orpheus` on `\$PATH` (will verify manually before marking ready)

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
EOF
)"

**What landed:**
- `orpheus-server` crate deleted; `orpheus serve` subcommand added in [crates/orpheus-cli/src/serve.rs](crates/orpheus-cli/src/serve.rs).
- Tauri bundler now embeds the `orpheus` binary via `externalBin` in [tauri.conf.json:34](crates/orpheus-tauri/src-tauri/tauri.conf.json#L34); helper [scripts/build-cli-for-tauri.sh](scripts/build-cli-for-tauri.sh) stages it per-triple.
- Cask templates add `binary "#{appdir}/Orpheus.app/Contents/MacOS/orpheus"` — one `brew install --cask orpheus` delivers both.
- CI updated across `latest`/`release`/`nightly`/`publish-crates`; mise tasks collapsed; docs updated.
- `mise run ci` green locally.

**To verify after CI runs:** a release-built `.dmg` installs and exposes `orpheus` on `$PATH`. I flagged this as an unchecked box in the PR's test plan.

content.text:
CI fully green. Before merging — the Tauri `.dmg` install path is the one thing still unverified (the cask `binary` stanza depends on Tauri placing the `orpheus` binary at `Orpheus.app/Contents/MacOS/orpheus`). If that path is wrong, the next push-to-main triggers the rolling cask publish and the `binary` stanza in the cask will 404 at install time.

Options:
1. **Merge now** — fast; if the cask breaks, revert the `binary` stanza in a follow-up commit to main. `latest`/`nightly` artifacts remain fine.
2. **Verify first** — I run `mise run tauri:build` locally (~3 min), confirm the `.dmg` has `orpheus` at the expected path, then merge.

Which? (I'd lean toward option 2 given how cheap it is, but your call.)
Running the Tauri bundle build. This will take a few minutes.

attachment.snippet:
1	# Orpheus
2	
3	![Orpheus](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/33de7189-3eaf-4239-b696-e03cac7c078a)
4	
5	> **καταβαίνω** — *to descend*
6	>
7	> Recover lost cryptocurrency from forgotten wallets.
8	
9	Orpheus is a Rust + Tauri v2 tool for extracting private keys from old Bitcoin
10	wallet files (Bitcoin Core `wallet.dat`, MultiBit Classic `.wallet`, Bitcoin
11	Core `dumpwallet` output, BIP39 seed phrases, and blockchain.com legacy
12	mnemonics). It ships as a single-binary CLI, an HTTP server hosting an embedded
13	React UI, and a native desktop app — all sharing the same extraction core.
14	
15	## Architecture
16	
17	```text
18	orpheus/
19	├── Cargo.toml              # workspace
20	├── mise.toml               # toolchain + tasks + hooks
21	├── rust-toolchain.toml
22	├── crates/
23	│   ├── orpheus-core/       # extractors, crypto, balance, scanner
24	│   ├── orpheus-cli/        # clap CLI: scan / extract / mnemonic / demo
25	│   ├── orpheus-server/     # axum + rust-embed: hosts apps/web/dist + /api
26	│   ├── orpheus-tauri/      # Tauri v2 desktop shell; same API, no sidecar
27	│   └── orpheus-demo-fixtures/
28	└── apps/
29	    └── web/                # Vite + React 19 + TS + Tailwind v4 + shadcn
30	```
31	
32	The `orpheus-core` crate is the single source of truth. Every frontend
33	(`cli`, `server`, `tauri`) delegates to it.
34	
35	## Getting started
36	
37	Orpheus is managed by **[mise](https://mise.jdx.dev)**. It's the single
38	source of truth for toolchain versions (see `[tools]` in `mise.toml`) and
39	for every scripted task — local development and CI run the same
40	`mise run <task>` commands.
41	
42	```bash
43	# Install mise once: https://mise.jdx.dev
44	mise install              # fetch rust 1.95, node 22, pnpm 9
45	mise run setup            # cargo fetch + pnpm install
46	```
47	
48	On `cd` into the repo mise auto-activates the toolchain and installs the
49	git pre-commit hook (`.githooks/pre-commit` → `mise run pre-commit` =
50	`cargo fmt --check` + `cargo clippy -D warnings`).
51	
52	### Common tasks
53	
54	```bash
55	mise run build           # cargo build --workspace
56	mise run test            # cargo test --workspace
57	mise run lint            # fmt --check + clippy + pnpm lint
58	mise run fmt             # cargo fmt + prettier
59	mise run cli:demo        # offline demo via CLI
60	mise run server:dev      # axum server on 127.0.0.1:3000
61	mise run web:dev         # Vite dev server on :5173 proxying /api → :3000
62	mise run dev             # both above together
63	mise run tauri:dev       # desktop app in development mode
64	mise run tauri:build     # package desktop app
65	```
66	
67	## Try the offline demo
68	
69	No wallets, no network. Runs against synthesized fixtures:
70	
71	```bash
72	cargo run -p orpheus-demo-fixtures  # regenerate fixtures/demo-wallets/
73	cargo run -p orpheus-cli -- demo
74	```
75	
76	Output: 5 wallets, 325 keys, a synthetic 0.08730104 BTC including a
77	homage to the 0.03865052 BTC recovered in the original session that
78	seeded this project.
79	
80	## Supported wallet formats
81	
82	| Format                                    | Extractor             |
83	|-------------------------------------------|-----------------------|
84	| Bitcoin Core `wallet.dat` (BDB or SQLite) | `bitcoin_core`        |
85	| MultiBit Classic `.wallet` (unencrypted)  | `multibit`            |
86	| MultiBit Classic `.wallet` (v3 scrypt+AES)| `encrypted`           |
87	| Bitcoin Core `dumpwallet` text            | `wallet_dump`         |
88	| Bitcoin Core `listdescriptors` JSON       | `wallet_dump`         |
89	| BIP39 mnemonic (12/15/18/21/24 words)     | `bip39_mnemonic`      |
90	| blockchain.com legacy 1626/65591 mnemonic | `blockchain_com`      |
91	
92	BIP39 derivation runs BIP44, BIP49, BIP84, *and* Breadwallet's legacy
93	`m/0'/{0,1}/x` — a first-class path anchored by a regression test for
94	the 2013-era iOS wallet that motivated this project.
95	
96	## Balance providers
97	
98	Every recovered address can be enriched with balance + received-total +
99	tx-count via `--provider`:
100	
101	| Value         | Source                                                        |
102	|---------------|---------------------------------------------------------------|
103	| `blockstream` | https://blockstream.info/api (public esplora, no API key) — **default** |
104	| `blockchain`  | https://blockchain.info/balance (public, batched up to 20 addrs) |
105	| `mock`        | offline lookup against `--mock-file` JSON (used by `orpheus demo`) |
106	| `none`        | skip balance lookup entirely                                  |
107	
108	The same identifiers work in `--provider <name>` on the CLI, the
109	`ORPHEUS_PROVIDER` env var, and the `Balance provider` dropdown in the
110	web UI. Use `none` or `mock` on air-gapped machines; the default
111	deliberately calls out to the internet so first-run scans report real
112	balances without extra flags.
113	
114	## Releases & versioning
115	
116	This repo follows [Conventional Commits](https://www.conventionalcommits.org/).
117	Release notes for every tagged release are generated from the commit
118	range and grouped by type (Features / Bug Fixes / Performance / …). See
119	[CONTRIBUTING.md](CONTRIBUTING.md#commit-messages--conventional-commits)
120	for the conventions we enforce.
121	
122	**Where to grab builds:**
123	
124	- [**Latest release**](https://github.com/dougnukem/orpheus/releases/latest) —
125	  the most recent signed, tagged release. Use this.
126	- [**`latest` pre-release**](https://github.com/dougnukem/orpheus/releases/tag/latest) —
127	  rolling build of `main`; useful for bleeding-edge smoke-testing.
128	
129	Artifacts published for every tagged release:
130	
131	- `orpheus-vX.Y.Z-{linux,macos,windows}-{x86_64,aarch64}.{tar.gz,zip}` —
132	  standalone `orpheus` CLI + `orpheus-server` binary
133	- Tauri desktop bundles (`.dmg` universal macOS, `.AppImage` + `.deb` Linux,
134	  `.msi` Windows)
135	- `SHA256SUMS` for integrity verification
136	
137	## Security
138	
139	- **Never feed real wallets to an untrusted build.** Audit the code first
140	  or pin a commit you've reviewed.
141	- **Offline by default.** The balance provider defaults to `mock` (fully
142	  offline); only opt-in to blockchain.info / blockstream.info.
143	- **Ephemeral Docker mode** is recommended for high-value recovery —
144	  mount the wallet directory read-only, run with `--network none` when
145	  not fetching balances, shut down when done.
146	- **Clear your scrollback** after a successful extraction. WIF keys in
147	  your terminal history are keys in your terminal history.
148

1	# Nightly — scheduled build of `main` producing a dated, signed
2	# pre-release.
3	#
4	# Complements `latest.yml`:
5	#   - `latest` (rolling): rebuilt on every push to main; useful for
6	#     always-fresh bleeding-edge binaries. Tag is force-replaced.
7	#   - `nightly-YYYY-MM-DD` (dated): rebuilt once per day at a fixed time;
8	#     useful when you want a stable reference point to pin to, reproduce
9	#     bugs against, or bisect regressions over several days.
10	#
11	# Retention: only the 7 most recent nightly-* releases are kept so the
12	# Releases page stays readable.
13	
14	name: nightly
15	
16	on:
17	  schedule:
18	    # 07:00 UTC = midnight PT, 2am CT, 3am ET. Pick a time when main is
19	    # least likely to be mid-push.
20	    - cron: "0 7 * * *"
21	  workflow_dispatch:
22	
23	permissions:
24	  contents: read
25	
26	concurrency:
27	  group: nightly
28	  cancel-in-progress: false
29	
30	env:
31	  CARGO_TERM_COLOR: always
32	
33	jobs:
34	  prepare:
35	    name: prepare nightly metadata
36	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
37	    outputs:
38	      tag: ${{ steps.meta.outputs.tag }}
39	      should_run: ${{ steps.meta.outputs.should_run }}
40	    steps:
41	      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
42	        with:
43	          fetch-depth: 2
44	      - name: compute tag + skip-if-no-changes
45	        id: meta
46	        env:
47	          EVENT_NAME: ${{ github.event_name }}
48	        shell: bash
49	        run: |
50	          set -euo pipefail
51	          TAG="nightly-$(date -u +%Y-%m-%d)"
52	          echo "tag=$TAG" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
53	          # On the scheduled run, skip if main hasn't moved in the last 24h
54	          # (otherwise we rebuild identical bits every day). Manual dispatch
55	          # always runs.
56	          if [[ "$EVENT_NAME" == "workflow_dispatch" ]]; then
57	            echo "should_run=true" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
58	            exit 0
59	          fi
60	          LAST_COMMIT_EPOCH=$(git log -1 --format=%ct origin/main)
61	          NOW=$(date -u +%s)
62	          if (( NOW - LAST_COMMIT_EPOCH > 86400 )); then
63	            echo "no commits in last 24h; skipping nightly"
64	            echo "should_run=false" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
65	          else
66	            echo "should_run=true" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
67	          fi
68	
69	  build-cli:
70	    name: cli / ${{ matrix.target }}
71	    needs: prepare
72	    if: needs.prepare.outputs.should_run == 'true'
73	    runs-on: ${{ matrix.os }}
74	    strategy:
75	      fail-fast: false
76	      matrix:
77	        include:
78	          - { target: x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu,  os: ubuntu-latest,    label: linux-x86_64,  archive: tar.gz }
79	          - { target: aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu, os: ubuntu-24.04-arm, label: linux-aarch64, archive: tar.gz }
80	          # macos-latest + cross-compile: macos-13 Intel runners unavailable.
81	          - { target: x86_64-apple-darwin,       os: macos-latest,     label: macos-x86_64,  archive: tar.gz }
82	          - { target: aarch64-apple-darwin,      os: macos-latest,     label: macos-aarch64, archive: tar.gz }
83	          - { target: x86_64-pc-windows-msvc,    os: windows-latest,   label: windows-x86_64, archive: zip }
84	    env:
85	      TAG: ${{ needs.prepare.outputs.tag }}
86	      TARGET: ${{ matrix.target }}
87	      LABEL: ${{ matrix.label }}
88	    steps:
89	      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
90	      - uses: jdx/mise-action@v2
91	        with:
92	          experimental: true
93	          install: true
94	          cache: true
95	      - shell: bash
96	        run: rustup target add "$TARGET"
97	      - uses: Swatinem/rust-cache@v2
98	        with:
99	          shared-key: nightly-cli-${{ matrix.target }}
100	
101	      - shell: bash
102	        run: cargo build --release --locked --target "$TARGET" -p orpheus-cli -p orpheus-server
103	
104	      - name: stage artifact (unix)
105	        if: runner.os != 'Windows'
106	        shell: bash
107	        run: |
108	          NAME="orpheus-$TAG-$LABEL"
109	          mkdir -p "staging/$NAME"
110	          cp "target/$TARGET/release/orpheus" "staging/$NAME/"
111	          cp "target/$TARGET/release/orpheus-server" "staging/$NAME/"
112	          cp README.md LICENSE "staging/$NAME/"
113	          ( cd staging && tar -czf "$NAME.tar.gz" "$NAME" )
114	          echo "ARTIFACT=$NAME" >> "$GITHUB_ENV"
115	
116	      - name: stage artifact (windows)
117	        if: runner.os == 'Windows'
118	        shell: pwsh
119	        run: |
120	          $NAME = "orpheus-$env:TAG-$env:LABEL"
121	          New-Item -ItemType Directory -Force -Path "staging/$NAME"
122	          Copy-Item "target/$env:TARGET/release/orpheus.exe" "staging/$NAME/"
123	          Copy-Item "target/$env:TARGET/release/orpheus-server.exe" "staging/$NAME/"
124	          Copy-Item README.md, LICENSE "staging/$NAME/"
125	          Compress-Archive -Path "staging/$NAME/*" -DestinationPath "staging/$NAME.zip"
126	          "ARTIFACT=$NAME" | Out-File -FilePath $env:GITHUB_ENV -Append
127	
128	      - uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
129	        with:
130	          name: ${{ env.ARTIFACT }}
131	          path: staging/${{ env.ARTIFACT }}.${{ matrix.archive }}
132	          if-no-files-found: error
133	
134	  build-tauri:
135	    name: tauri / ${{ matrix.name }}
136	    needs: prepare
137	    if: needs.prepare.outputs.should_run == 'true'
138	    runs-on: ${{ matrix.os }}
139	    strategy:
140	      fail-fast: false
141	      matrix:
142	        include:
143	          - { name: macos-universal, os: macos-latest,   args: "--target universal-apple-darwin", rust-targets: "aarch64-apple-darwin,x86_64-apple-darwin" }
144	          - { name: linux-x86_64,    os: ubuntu-22.04,   args: "",                                rust-targets: "" }
145	          - { name: windows-x86_64,  os: windows-latest, args: "",                                rust-targets: "" }
146	    env:
147	      RUST_TARGETS: ${{ matrix.rust-targets }}
148	    steps:
149	      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
150	      - uses: jdx/mise-action@v2
151	        with:
152	          experimental: true
153	          install: true
154	          cache: true
155	      - name: add rust targets
156	        if: matrix.rust-targets != ''
157	        shell: bash
158	        run: |
159	          IFS=',' read -r -a targets <<< "$RUST_TARGETS"
160	          for t in "${targets[@]}"; do rustup target add "$t"; done
161	      - uses: Swatinem/rust-cache@v2
162	        with:
163	          workspaces: "crates/orpheus-tauri/src-tauri -> target"
164	          shared-key: nightly-tauri-${{ matrix.name }}
165	
166	      - uses: ./.github/actions/install-linux-deps
167	
168	      - run: mise run web:install
169	      - uses: tauri-apps/tauri-action@v0
170	        env:
171	          GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
172	        with:
173	          projectPath: crates/orpheus-tauri/src-tauri
174	          args: ${{ matrix.args }}
175	
176	      - name: collect tauri bundles
177	        shell: bash
178	        run: |
179	          set -euo pipefail
180	          mkdir -p tauri-output
181	          # Cargo workspace: bundles land under the workspace root `target/`.
182	          # Layout varies by target triple:
183	          #   native   -> target/release/bundle/<kind>/...
184	          #   crossed  -> target/<triple>/release/bundle/<kind>/...
185	          #               (e.g. target/universal-apple-darwin/release/bundle/...)
186	          # So search the whole target/ tree and filter by the
187	          # .../release/bundle/ path segment. Tauri v2 emits duplicates
188	          # (Orpheus_0.1.0_amd64.deb AND lowercase
189	          # orpheus_0.1.0_amd64.deb from the Cargo crate name); the
190	          # Orpheus_/Orpheus- name filter keeps only the productName-cased
191	          # ones.
192	          find target -type f -path "*/release/bundle/*" \
193	            \( -name "Orpheus_*.dmg"    -o -name "Orpheus*.app.tar.gz" \
194	               -o -name "Orpheus_*.AppImage" \
195	               -o -name "Orpheus_*.deb" -o -name "Orpheus-*.rpm" \
196	               -o -name "Orpheus_*.msi" -o -name "Orpheus_*.exe" \) \
197	            -exec cp {} tauri-output/ \;

1	# Orpheus — developer environment managed by mise (https://mise.jdx.dev).
2	#
3	# mise is the single source of truth for tool versions and for every
4	# scripted task. The same `mise run <task>` commands are invoked from
5	# local shells, git hooks, and CI (see .github/workflows/*.yml).
6	#
7	# On `cd` into the repo (with mise's shell integration active) the pinned
8	# toolchain versions auto-activate. Run `mise install` to fetch them the
9	# first time.
10	
11	[tools]
12	rust = "1.95"
13	node = "22"
14	pnpm = "9"
15	# Tauri build prerequisites on macOS are Xcode Command Line Tools; on Linux
16	# users need libwebkit2gtk-4.1-dev + friends (see .github/workflows/ci.yml).
17	
18	[env]
19	# rustup-managed toolchain always wins over /opt/homebrew/bin/{cargo,rustc}.
20	# Windows CI runners don't set $HOME — fall back to %USERPROFILE% there so
21	# mise can still render the template.
22	_.path = [
23	    "{% if env.HOME %}{{ env.HOME }}/.cargo/bin{% elif env.USERPROFILE %}{{ env.USERPROFILE }}/.cargo/bin{% else %}{{ config_root }}/.cargo/bin{% endif %}",
24	]
25	RUST_BACKTRACE = "1"
26	RUST_LOG       = "orpheus=info,tower_http=info"
27	
28	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
29	# Lifecycle hooks
30	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
31	# "enter" fires once per shell when mise activates this project. We use it
32	# to install git hooks on the first entry of a fresh clone.
33	[hooks]
34	enter = "test -x .git/hooks/pre-commit || mise run hooks:install"
35	
36	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
37	# Setup / housekeeping
38	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
39	
40	[tasks.setup]
41	description = "Install all Rust + frontend dependencies"
42	run = [
43	    "cargo fetch --locked || cargo fetch",
44	    "mise run web:install",
45	]
46	
47	[tasks."hooks:install"]
48	description = "Symlink git hooks from .githooks/ into .git/hooks/"
49	run = """
50	set -e
51	mkdir -p .git/hooks
52	for hook in .githooks/*; do
53	    name=$(basename "$hook")
54	    ln -sf "../../$hook" ".git/hooks/$name"
55	done
56	echo "git hooks installed from .githooks/"
57	"""
58	
59	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
60	# Rust
61	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
62	
63	[tasks.build]
64	description = "Build the whole Rust workspace (debug)"
65	run = "cargo build --workspace --all-targets"
66	
67	[tasks."build:release"]
68	description = "Release build of the CLI + server binaries into target/release"
69	run = "cargo build --release --locked -p orpheus-cli -p orpheus-server"
70	
71	[tasks.install]
72	description = "Install `orpheus` + `orpheus-server` into ~/.cargo/bin"
73	run = "cargo install --path crates/orpheus-cli --locked --force && cargo install --path crates/orpheus-server --locked --force"
74	
75	[tasks.test]
76	description = "Run all Rust tests"
77	# orpheus-tauri has no tests of its own. clippy --workspace compile-checks
78	# it and latest.yml handles the full tauri build for releases.
79	run = "cargo test --workspace --locked --exclude orpheus-tauri"
80	
81	[tasks."test:core"]
82	description = "Run orpheus-core tests only"
83	run = "cargo test -p orpheus-core --locked"
84	
85	[tasks."rust:lint"]
86	description = "Cargo fmt --check + clippy"
87	run = [
88	    "cargo fmt --all -- --check",
89	    "cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings",
90	]
91	
92	[tasks."rust:fmt"]
93	description = "Auto-format Rust sources"
94	run = "cargo fmt --all"
95	
96	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
97	# Frontend (apps/web)
98	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
99	
100	[tasks."web:install"]
101	description = "Install apps/web dependencies with pnpm"
102	dir = "apps/web"
103	run = "pnpm install --frozen-lockfile || pnpm install"
104	
105	[tasks."web:dev"]
106	description = "Vite dev server on :5173 proxying /api -> :3000"
107	dir = "apps/web"
108	run = "pnpm dev"
109	
110	[tasks."web:build"]
111	description = "Build React app into apps/web/dist"
112	dir = "apps/web"
113	run = "pnpm build"
114	
115	[tasks."web:lint"]
116	description = "Run ESLint on the frontend"
117	depends = ["web:install"]
118	dir = "apps/web"
119	run = "pnpm lint"
120	
121	[tasks."web:typecheck"]
122	description = "Run the TS compiler without emitting"
123	depends = ["web:install"]
124	dir = "apps/web"
125	run = "pnpm typecheck"
126	
127	[tasks."web:fmt"]
128	description = "Prettier write for frontend sources"
129	dir = "apps/web"
130	run = "pnpm format"
131	
132	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
133	# Composite / meta tasks
134	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
135	
136	[tasks.lint]
137	description = "Full lint: Rust fmt/clippy + frontend lint + typecheck"
138	depends = ["rust:lint", "web:lint", "web:typecheck"]
139	
140	[tasks.fmt]
141	description = "Auto-format everything"
142	depends = ["rust:fmt", "web:fmt"]
143	
144	[tasks.pre-commit]
145	description = "Fast checks for the git pre-commit hook"
146	depends = ["rust:lint"]
147	
148	[tasks.ci]
149	description = "What CI runs on every pull request"
150	depends = ["lint", "test", "web:build", "cli:demo"]
151	
152	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
153	# Running the app
154	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
155	
156	[tasks."server:dev"]
157	description = "Axum server on 127.0.0.1:3000 serving embedded web/dist"
158	run = "cargo run -p orpheus-server -- --host 127.0.0.1 --port 3000"
159	
160	[tasks.dev]
161	description = "Run axum (:3000) + Vite (:5173) together"
162	run = [
163	    { task = "server:dev" },
164	    { task = "web:dev" },
165	]
166	
167	[tasks."cli:demo"]
168	description = "Run the bundled offline demo scan (synthetic fixtures)"
169	run = "cargo run -p orpheus-cli --locked -- demo"
170	
171	[tasks."demo:fixtures"]
172	description = "Regenerate synthetic demo wallet fixtures"
173	run = "cargo run -p orpheus-demo-fixtures --locked"
174	
175	[tasks."tauri:dev"]
176	description = "Tauri v2 desktop app in development mode"
177	dir = "crates/orpheus-tauri"
178	run = "pnpm tauri dev"
179	
180	[tasks."tauri:build"]
181	description = "Package the Tauri desktop app for the current platform"
182	dir = "crates/orpheus-tauri"
183	run = "pnpm tauri build"
184	
185	[tasks."release:setup-gpg"]
186	description = "One-time: generate the Orpheus release signing key + sync to GitHub secrets + .secrets"
187	# raw = true preserves the controlling TTY so the script's silent
188	# passphrase prompt (read -s) works.
189	raw = true
190	run = "./scripts/setup-gpg-release-key.sh"

1	# Claude / Agent operating instructions for Orpheus
2	
3	These notes teach a coding agent how to work productively in this repo.
4	They supplement any global `~/.claude/CLAUDE.md`.
5	
6	## Mental model
7	
8	Orpheus is a **Rust workspace** (`crates/`) plus a **React web app**
9	(`apps/web`) that the Rust server and Tauri shell both embed. A single
10	extraction core is shared by three frontends:
11	
12	```text
13	apps/web (Vite + React + TS)          user-facing UI — bundled into dist/
14	                                      consumed by both server and tauri
15	
16	crates/
17	  orpheus-core           ← the one source of truth for crypto/extractors/
18	                           balance/scanner. Every other crate depends on it.
19	  orpheus-cli            ← clap: scan / extract / mnemonic / demo
20	  orpheus-server         ← axum; serves embedded apps/web/dist + JSON API
21	  orpheus-tauri/src-tauri← Tauri v2 desktop shell; #[tauri::command]s call
22	                           directly into orpheus-core (no HTTP sidecar)
23	  orpheus-demo-fixtures  ← regenerates synthesized fake wallets +
24	                           fixtures/mock_balances.json
25	```
26	
27	Every production code path in `orpheus-core` has a companion test that
28	**pins the output to a known value** — BIP test vector, fixed WIF,
29	decrypt-roundtrip with a hardcoded plaintext. That discipline is
30	non-negotiable; see "Security-sensitive changes" below.
31	
32	## Toolchain
33	
34	**`mise` is the only supported toolchain manager.** `mise.toml` pins:
35	
36	- rust 1.95
37	- node 22
38	- pnpm 9
39	
40	CI invokes `mise run <task>` exactly like a developer does; if you add a
41	build/test/lint step, add it to `mise.toml` and reference it from both
42	local hooks and `.github/workflows/*.yml`.
43	
44	Never invoke `npm` directly — only `pnpm`. Never call the system
45	`rustc`/`cargo` before `$HOME/.cargo/bin` — `mise.toml` puts the rustup
46	toolchain on PATH for this reason.
47	
48	## Workflow
49	
50	**Every change ships via a feature branch and a pull request** — no
51	direct commits to `main`.
52	
53	1. `git switch -c <type>/<short-name>` (e.g. `feat/bip84-accounts`,
54	   `fix/multibit-salt-scan`).
55	2. Commits use [Conventional Commits](https://www.conventionalcommits.org/).
56	   Types: `feat`, `fix`, `perf`, `refactor`, `docs`, `test`, `build`,
57	   `ci`, `chore`, `revert`. Optional `(scope)`: `core`, `cli`, `server`,
58	   `tauri`, `web`, `ci`, `deps`, `fixtures`. `!` before `:` for
59	   breaking changes.
60	3. Push early, open a draft PR so CI runs.
61	4. `mise run ci` must pass locally before requesting review.
62	5. A reviewer plus green CI are required before merging. Squash-merge
63	   keeps the PR title as the merge commit — keep it conventional.
64	
65	## Tasks (single source of truth: `mise.toml`)
66	
67	| Task                    | Use when                                      |
68	|-------------------------|-----------------------------------------------|
69	| `mise run setup`        | fresh clone — fetches all deps                |
70	| `mise run ci`           | full pre-PR check (what GitHub CI also runs)  |
71	| `mise run lint`         | fmt --check + clippy + web lint + typecheck   |
72	| `mise run fmt`          | auto-format everything                        |
73	| `mise run test`         | `cargo test --workspace --locked`             |
74	| `mise run demo:fixtures`| regenerate synthetic demo wallets             |
75	| `mise run cli:demo`     | offline scan of the demo fixtures             |
76	| `mise run server:dev`   | axum on 127.0.0.1:3000                        |
77	| `mise run web:dev`      | Vite on :5173 proxying /api → :3000           |
78	| `mise run dev`          | server + web together                         |
79	| `mise run tauri:dev`    | desktop app in development                    |
80	
81	## Security-sensitive changes
82	
83	**Anything touching `crypto`, `extractors`, or `balance` requires a test
84	that pins the behaviour to a known vector.** Acceptable pinning:
85	
86	- Published BIP test vectors (BIP32/39/49/84/173/350).
87	- Round-trip fixtures generated inside the same test (encrypt → assert →
88	  decrypt → assert equal).
89	- Decode-test payloads with asserted outputs.
90	
91	**Never put real wallets, real mnemonics, or real passwords in the repo
92	— even in tests under `fixtures/`.** `orpheus-demo-fixtures` generates
93	everything from a throwaway BIP39 test mnemonic; use it as the template
94	for new fixture needs. `.gitignore` blocks `*.wallet`, `*.dat`,
95	`*.aes.json`, and `passwords*.txt` outside `fixtures/` — keep it that way.
96	
97	## Wallet format support (as of v0.1)
98	
99	| Format                                    | Extractor             |
100	|-------------------------------------------|-----------------------|
101	| Bitcoin Core `wallet.dat` (BDB / SQLite)  | `bitcoin_core`        |
102	| MultiBit Classic `.wallet` (unencrypted)  | `multibit`            |
103	| MultiBit Classic `.wallet` (v3 scrypt+AES)| `encrypted`           |
104	| Bitcoin Core `dumpwallet` text            | `wallet_dump`         |
105	| Bitcoin Core `listdescriptors` JSON       | `wallet_dump`         |
106	| BIP39 (12/15/18/21/24 words)              | `bip39_mnemonic`      |
107	| blockchain.com legacy 1626/65591 mnemonic | `blockchain_com`      |
108	
109	BIP39 derivation always covers BIP44, BIP49, BIP84 **and Breadwallet**
110	`m/0'/{0,1}/x`. The Breadwallet path is load-bearing — it's anchored by
111	a regression test in `crates/orpheus-core/src/extractors/bip39_mnemonic.rs`
112	that traces back to the 2013-era iOS wallet recovery that seeded this
113	project. Don't remove it.
114	
115	## Balance providers
116	
117	Default is `blockstream`. Full list (kept in sync across
118	`orpheus-core::balance::VALID_PROVIDERS`, clap `CliProvider`, and the
119	frontend `<select>`):
120	
121	- `blockstream` — public esplora, no API key
122	- `blockchain` — blockchain.info, batched up to 20 addresses
123	- `mock` — offline lookup against `--mock-file` JSON
124	- `none` — skip balance lookup (all zeros)
125	
126	If you add a provider, add it to all three surfaces in the same PR.
127	
128	## CI / release pipeline
129	
130	Three workflows under `.github/workflows/`:
131	
132	- **`ci.yml`** — pull requests + pushes to main. Matrix test across
133	  ubuntu/macos/windows plus a `demo-smoke` job that asserts the 0.03865052
134	  BTC homage still prints in the offline demo. Aggregated into an
135	  `all-green` job for branch-protection.
136	- **`latest.yml`** — push to main. Builds the CLI + server + Tauri
137	  desktop bundles across the native-runner matrix, atomically
138	  replaces the `latest` GitHub prerelease with every artifact. The
139	  `latest` release is `prerelease: true` so the canonical "Latest
140	  release" pointer stays on tagged versions.
141	- **`release.yml`** — tag `v*` or manual `workflow_dispatch`. Draft
142	  release → matrix builds → upload → `gh release edit --draft=false
143	  --latest`. Nothing is visible to users until the last step.
144	
145	`contents: write` is scoped to the release/publish jobs only. All
146	`${{ github.* }}` and `inputs.*` values that flow into shell bodies are
147	routed through `env:` blocks per the workflow-injection guidance.
148	
149	Dependabot keeps third-party actions, Cargo crates, and npm packages
150	current. Action-version bumps rewrite floating tags to full-SHA pins
151	automatically.
152	
153	## Writing tests
154	
155	- Unit tests live in the same file as the code (`#[cfg(test)] mod
156	  tests { ... }`).
157	- Don't hit the network. `MockProvider` is the correct test seam for
158	  balance code.
159	- Prefer a single failing pinned assertion to a wall of looser sanity
160	  checks — it makes regressions obvious.
161	- When you add a new extractor, add at least: `can_handle` true-positive,
162	  `can_handle` false-positive, `extract` success path, and
163	  `extract` failure path.
164	
165	## Commit / PR etiquette for agents
166	
167	- If you are spawned to do work, open a draft PR; do not push straight
168	  to `main`.
169	- Leave a one-sentence "Test plan" in the PR body noting which

1	# Publish crates to crates.io — called by release.yml after a tagged
2	# release is live on GitHub.
3	#
4	# Only publishes library + binary crates that have `publish = true`
5	# (the default). `orpheus-tauri` and `orpheus-demo-fixtures` are marked
6	# `publish = false` and skipped automatically by cargo.
7	#
8	# Order matters: orpheus-core must publish before orpheus-cli and
9	# orpheus-server because those depend on it via path + version. Cargo
10	# usually discovers this via `cargo publish --workspace`, but we publish
11	# explicitly for predictability and better error reporting.
12	
13	name: publish-crates
14	
15	on:
16	  workflow_call:
17	    inputs:
18	      tag:
19	        description: "Release tag (e.g. v0.1.1). The crate version must match tag-less."
20	        required: true
21	        type: string
22	  workflow_dispatch:
23	    inputs:
24	      tag:
25	        description: "Release tag (e.g. v0.1.1)."
26	        required: true
27	        type: string
28	      dry_run:
29	        description: "If true, run cargo publish --dry-run only."
30	        required: false
31	        type: boolean
32	        default: false
33	
34	permissions:
35	  contents: read
36	
37	concurrency:
38	  group: publish-crates-${{ inputs.tag }}
39	  cancel-in-progress: false
40	
41	jobs:
42	  publish:
43	    name: cargo publish
44	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
45	    env:
46	      TAG: ${{ inputs.tag }}
47	      DRY_RUN: ${{ inputs.dry_run || 'false' }}
48	      CARGO_REGISTRY_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.CARGO_REGISTRY_TOKEN }}
49	    steps:
50	      - name: check CARGO_REGISTRY_TOKEN is set
51	        id: gate
52	        env:
53	          TOKEN: ${{ secrets.CARGO_REGISTRY_TOKEN }}
54	        shell: bash
55	        run: |
56	          if [[ -z "$TOKEN" ]]; then
57	            echo "::warning::CARGO_REGISTRY_TOKEN not set; skipping crates.io publish"
58	            echo "skip=true" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
59	          else
60	            echo "skip=false" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
61	          fi
62	
63	      - if: steps.gate.outputs.skip == 'false'
64	        uses: actions/checkout@v4
65	        with:
66	          ref: ${{ inputs.tag }}
67	          fetch-depth: 0
68	
69	      - if: steps.gate.outputs.skip == 'false'
70	        uses: jdx/mise-action@v2
71	        with:
72	          experimental: true
73	          install: true
74	          cache: true
75	
76	      - if: steps.gate.outputs.skip == 'false'
77	        uses: Swatinem/rust-cache@v2
78	        with:
79	          shared-key: publish-crates
80	
81	      - if: steps.gate.outputs.skip == 'false'
82	        name: verify crate versions match the tag
83	        shell: bash
84	        run: |
85	          set -euo pipefail
86	          EXPECTED="${TAG#v}"
87	          WORKSPACE_VERSION=$(grep -E '^version\s*=' Cargo.toml | head -1 | sed -E 's/.*"([^"]+)".*/\1/')
88	          echo "tag: $TAG  -> expected crate version: $EXPECTED"
89	          echo "workspace version: $WORKSPACE_VERSION"
90	          if [[ "$EXPECTED" != "$WORKSPACE_VERSION" ]]; then
91	            echo "::error::tag ($EXPECTED) does not match workspace.version ($WORKSPACE_VERSION)"
92	            exit 1
93	          fi
94	
95	      - name: cargo publish (dry-run)
96	        if: steps.gate.outputs.skip == 'false' && env.DRY_RUN == 'true'
97	        shell: bash
98	        run: |
99	          set -euo pipefail
100	          for c in orpheus-core orpheus-cli orpheus-server; do
101	            echo "=== dry-run: $c ==="
102	            cargo publish --dry-run -p "$c"
103	          done
104	
105	      - name: cargo publish (real)
106	        if: steps.gate.outputs.skip == 'false' && env.DRY_RUN != 'true'
107	        shell: bash
108	        run: |
109	          set -euo pipefail
110	          # Publish in dependency order. Between crates, pause briefly to let
111	          # the crates.io index catch up so dependents can resolve the new
112	          # version. `--no-verify` on dependents avoids a redundant full build.
113	          publish_one() {
114	            local crate=$1
115	            shift
116	            echo "=== publishing: $crate ==="
117	            # Allow failure only for the specific "already uploaded" case so
118	            # re-runs are idempotent.
119	            if ! cargo publish -p "$crate" "$@"; then
120	              if cargo search "$crate" | grep -q "^$crate = \"${TAG#v}\""; then
121	                echo "$crate@${TAG#v} already on crates.io; skipping"
122	                return 0
123	              fi
124	              return 1
125	            fi
126	          }
127	          publish_one orpheus-core
128	          # Give crates.io a moment to index the new version.
129	          sleep 30
130	          publish_one orpheus-cli --no-verify
131	          publish_one orpheus-server --no-verify

1	# Releasing Orpheus
2	
3	This doc is the runbook for cutting a release, the signing setup behind
4	it, and the placeholders that turn on platform-specific signing when
5	their accounts become available.
6	
7	## Release channels
8	
9	| Channel                 | Workflow              | Trigger                       | Tag shape            | GitHub "latest"? |
10	|-------------------------|-----------------------|-------------------------------|----------------------|------------------|
11	| Stable                  | `release.yml`         | push `v*` tag / manual        | `v0.1.0`             | ✅ yes           |
12	| Rolling pre-release     | `latest.yml`          | push to `main`                | `latest`             | ❌ pre-release   |
13	| Nightly (dated)         | `nightly.yml`         | cron `0 7 * * *` / manual     | `nightly-YYYY-MM-DD` | ❌ pre-release   |
14	
15	`latest` rebuilds on every push, `nightly-*` rebuilds once a day. Both
16	are pre-releases so GitHub's "Latest release" pointer only ever moves
17	when a stable `v*` tag is cut.
18	
19	## Cutting a stable release
20	
21	```bash
22	# 1. Bump versions + run CI locally.
23	#    The single source of truth is `version = "X.Y.Z"` in Cargo.toml
24	#    under [workspace.package]. All crates inherit it via
25	#    `version.workspace = true`, so one edit covers every crate.
26	#    Use cargo-release for the tag+commit in one go:
27	cargo install cargo-release   # one-time
28	cargo release 0.1.1 --workspace --execute --no-publish --no-push
29	
30	# 2. Push the commit + tag. The tag push triggers release.yml.
31	git push origin main --follow-tags
32	```
33	
34	The `release.yml` workflow will:
35	
36	1. Create a draft GitHub release.
37	2. Build CLI + server binaries for 5 targets; build Tauri bundles for
38	   macOS (universal), Linux x86_64, Windows x86_64.
39	3. GPG-sign every artifact and produce a detached-signed `SHA256SUMS`.
40	4. Attach artifacts to the draft and flip it to published + latest.
41	5. Publish `orpheus-core`, `orpheus-cli`, `orpheus-server` to crates.io.
42	6. Update the Homebrew cask in `dougnukem/homebrew-orpheus`.
43	
44	Failure anywhere in steps 1–4 leaves the draft untouched — re-run the
45	workflow. Failure in 5 or 6 does not block the GitHub release; rerun the
46	specific sub-workflow (`publish-crates` / `publish-cask`) manually via
47	`gh workflow run`.
48	
49	## Linux self-signing (GPG)
50	
51	All Linux artifacts — plus the tarballs, zips, DMGs, and MSIs — are
52	signed with a long-lived GPG signing key. Users verify with:
53	
54	```bash
55	curl -L https://github.com/dougnukem/orpheus/raw/main/packaging/gpg/orpheus-release-pubkey.asc \
56	  | gpg --import
57	
58	# Per-artifact
59	gpg --verify orpheus_0.1.0_amd64.deb.asc orpheus_0.1.0_amd64.deb
60	
61	# Or one-shot via the signed manifest
62	gpg --verify SHA256SUMS.asc SHA256SUMS
63	sha256sum -c SHA256SUMS
64	```
65	
66	### One-time key setup
67	
68	```bash
69	# 1. Generate a 4096-bit RSA master + signing subkey. On the interactive
70	#    prompts pick: RSA+RSA, 4096 bits, expiry 2y.
71	gpg --full-generate-key
72	#    UID: "Orpheus Release Signing <releases@orpheus.dev>"
73	
74	# 2. Identify the key IDs.
75	gpg --list-secret-keys --keyid-format=LONG
76	
77	# 3. Export the public key into the repo.
78	gpg --armor --export <MASTER_KEY_ID> \
79	  > packaging/gpg/orpheus-release-pubkey.asc
80	
81	# 4. Export ONLY the signing subkey (never the master) for CI. The `!`
82	#    after the subkey ID tells gpg to export that subkey only.
83	gpg --armor --export-secret-subkeys <SUBKEY_ID>! | base64 > ci-key.b64
84	
85	# 5. Load into GitHub secrets:
86	gh secret set GPG_PRIVATE_KEY < ci-key.b64
87	gh secret set GPG_PASSPHRASE --body "<subkey passphrase>"
88	gh secret set GPG_KEY_ID     --body "<SUBKEY_ID>"
89	rm ci-key.b64
90	
91	# 6. Publish the public key to a keyserver so casual users can find it.
92	gpg --keyserver keys.openpgp.org --send-keys <MASTER_KEY_ID>
93	```
94	
95	### Key storage best practices
96	
97	- Keep the **master key offline** (paper backup + air-gapped USB stick).
98	  Rotate the subkey yearly; the master stays stable for 2+ years.
99	- CI only ever holds the signing subkey. Losing it means revoking the
100	  subkey and issuing a new one — not rotating the whole identity.
101	- For the highest-value stable releases, consider moving the master key
102	  onto a YubiKey and signing manually from a local machine before
103	  uploading. The rolling `latest`/`nightly` CI signatures stay automated.
104	
105	## crates.io publishing
106	
107	The tagged `release.yml` calls `publish-crates.yml`. Bootstrap once:
108	
109	```bash
110	# 1. Generate a token scoped to publishing the three crates (NOT account-wide).
111	#    Visit https://crates.io/settings/tokens -> "New token".
112	#    Scopes: publish-new, publish-update
113	#    Crate filter: orpheus-core, orpheus-cli, orpheus-server
114	
115	gh secret set CARGO_REGISTRY_TOKEN --body "cio_..."
116	
117	# 2. Enable 2FA on your crates.io account.
118	```
119	
120	Publishing order (handled automatically by the workflow):
121	
122	1. `orpheus-core`
123	2. `orpheus-cli`   (depends on core; `--no-verify` to skip redundant rebuild)
124	3. `orpheus-server` (same)
125	
126	`orpheus-tauri` and `orpheus-demo-fixtures` are marked `publish = false`
127	and skipped.
128	
129	Users can install without compiling via
130	[`cargo-binstall`](https://github.com/cargo-bins/cargo-binstall), which
131	downloads the matching archive from GitHub Releases:
132	
133	```bash
134	cargo binstall orpheus-cli
135	```
136	
137	The `[package.metadata.binstall]` block in each crate's `Cargo.toml`
138	points at the release artifact naming scheme.
139	
140	## Homebrew cask
141	
142	The first release must bootstrap the tap by hand; afterwards
143	`publish-cask.yml` keeps it current automatically.
144	
145	```bash
146	# 1. Create the tap repo.
147	gh repo create dougnukem/homebrew-orpheus --public --add-readme \
148	  --description "Homebrew tap for Orpheus"
149	cd /tmp && git clone git@github.com:dougnukem/homebrew-orpheus.git
150	cd homebrew-orpheus && mkdir Casks
151	printf 'cask "orpheus" do\n  version "0.0.0"\nend\n' > Casks/orpheus.rb
152	git add . && git commit -m "init tap" && git push
153	
154	# 2. Create a PAT scoped to the tap repo and store as HOMEBREW_TAP_TOKEN.
155	#    Fine-grained token, repo: dougnukem/homebrew-orpheus, permissions:
156	#    Contents: Read and write.
157	gh secret set HOMEBREW_TAP_TOKEN --body "<pat>"
158	```
159	
160	Users then install with:
161	
162	```bash
163	brew tap dougnukem/orpheus
164	brew install --cask orpheus
165	```
166	
167	The cask template ships a `caveats` block explaining the one-time
168	`xattr -cr /Applications/Orpheus.app` required on unsigned builds.
169	Delete that block from the two templates in
170	[packaging/homebrew/](../packaging/homebrew/) once Apple signing goes
171	live.
172	
173	## Windows signing — SignPath Foundation (placeholder)
174	
175	The release workflow contains a commented-out SignPath block ready to
176	enable once the Foundation application is approved.
177	
178	### Application checklist
179	
180	- MIT/Apache-2.0 license (✅ already MIT)
181	- Public repo with non-trivial history (✅)
182	- Tagged release + changelog (✅ once the first v* tag is cut)
183	- Reproducible build via GitHub Actions (✅)
184	- Homepage describing the app (✅ the README)
185	
186	Apply at [signpath.io/foundation](https://about.signpath.io/foundation).
187	Review takes ~1–2 weeks.
188	
189	### After approval
190	
191	1. On signpath.io: create project `orpheus`, signing policy
192	   `release-signing`, and link it to this repo.
193	2. Create an API token (`Settings → API Tokens → Add`) and store it:
194	
195	   ```bash
196	   gh secret set  SIGNPATH_API_TOKEN --body "<token>"
197	   gh variable set SIGNPATH_ORG_ID   --body "<org-uuid>"
198	   ```
199	
200	3. Uncomment the `signpath/github-action-submit-signing-request@v1` steps
201	   in `release.yml` (both `build-cli` and `build-tauri` jobs).
202	4. Remove any Windows-signing disclaimers from the README.
203	
204	SignPath signs unsigned artifacts uploaded to GitHub Actions, then

1	# Latest — continuous pre-release tracking `main`.
2	#
3	# On every push to main:
4	#   1. Build CLI + server binaries across native-runner matrix.
5	#   2. Build Tauri desktop bundles via tauri-action.
6	#   3. Atomically publish/replace the `latest` PRE-release on GitHub with
7	#      every artifact attached. The `latest` release is marked prerelease
8	#      so GitHub's "Latest release" pointer never tracks it — that pointer
9	#      is reserved for tagged releases created by release.yml.
10	#
11	# `${{ github.sha }}` etc. are bound into the job via env: blocks and then
12	# referenced as shell variables to neutralise any scripting-injection risk.
13	
14	name: latest
15	
16	on:
17	  push:
18	    branches: [main]
19	  workflow_dispatch:
20	
21	permissions:
22	  contents: read
23	
24	concurrency:
25	  group: latest
26	  cancel-in-progress: true
27	
28	env:
29	  CARGO_TERM_COLOR: always
30	  LATEST_TAG: latest
31	
32	jobs:
33	  build-cli:
34	    name: cli / ${{ matrix.target }}
35	    runs-on: ${{ matrix.os }}
36	    strategy:
37	      fail-fast: false
38	      matrix:
39	        include:
40	          - target: x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu
41	            os: ubuntu-latest
42	            artifact: orpheus-linux-x86_64
43	            archive: tar.gz
44	          - target: aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu
45	            os: ubuntu-24.04-arm
46	            artifact: orpheus-linux-aarch64
47	            archive: tar.gz
48	          # Cross-compile x86_64 binaries from the Apple Silicon runner —
49	          # GitHub's macos-13 (Intel) runner pool is effectively unavailable
50	          # (jobs queue for hours and never start). `rustup target add` +
51	          # `cargo build --target x86_64-apple-darwin` on macos-latest
52	          # produces the same artifact.
53	          - target: x86_64-apple-darwin
54	            os: macos-latest
55	            artifact: orpheus-macos-x86_64
56	            archive: tar.gz
57	          - target: aarch64-apple-darwin
58	            os: macos-latest
59	            artifact: orpheus-macos-aarch64
60	            archive: tar.gz
61	          - target: x86_64-pc-windows-msvc
62	            os: windows-latest
63	            artifact: orpheus-windows-x86_64
64	            archive: zip
65	    env:
66	      TARGET: ${{ matrix.target }}
67	      ARTIFACT: ${{ matrix.artifact }}
68	    steps:
69	      - uses: actions/checkout@v6
70	      - uses: jdx/mise-action@v4
71	        with:
72	          experimental: true
73	          install: true
74	          cache: true
75	      - name: add rust target
76	        shell: bash
77	        run: rustup target add "$TARGET"
78	      - uses: Swatinem/rust-cache@v2
79	        with:
80	          shared-key: latest-cli-${{ matrix.target }}
81	
82	      - name: build release binaries
83	        shell: bash
84	        run: cargo build --release --locked --target "$TARGET" -p orpheus-cli -p orpheus-server
85	
86	      - name: stage artifact (unix)
87	        if: runner.os != 'Windows'
88	        shell: bash
89	        run: |
90	          mkdir -p "staging/$ARTIFACT"
91	          cp "target/$TARGET/release/orpheus" "staging/$ARTIFACT/"
92	          cp "target/$TARGET/release/orpheus-server" "staging/$ARTIFACT/"
93	          cp README.md LICENSE "staging/$ARTIFACT/"
94	          ( cd staging && tar -czf "$ARTIFACT.tar.gz" "$ARTIFACT" )
95	
96	      - name: stage artifact (windows)
97	        if: runner.os == 'Windows'
98	        shell: pwsh
99	        run: |
100	          New-Item -ItemType Directory -Force -Path "staging/$env:ARTIFACT"
101	          Copy-Item "target/$env:TARGET/release/orpheus.exe" "staging/$env:ARTIFACT/"
102	          Copy-Item "target/$env:TARGET/release/orpheus-server.exe" "staging/$env:ARTIFACT/"
103	          Copy-Item README.md, LICENSE "staging/$env:ARTIFACT/"
104	          Compress-Archive -Path "staging/$env:ARTIFACT/*" -DestinationPath "staging/$env:ARTIFACT.zip"
105	
106	      - uses: actions/upload-artifact@v7
107	        with:
108	          name: ${{ matrix.artifact }}
109	          path: staging/${{ matrix.artifact }}.${{ matrix.archive }}
110	          if-no-files-found: error
111	
112	  build-tauri:
113	    name: tauri / ${{ matrix.name }}
114	    runs-on: ${{ matrix.os }}
115	    strategy:
116	      fail-fast: false
117	      matrix:
118	        include:
119	          - name: macos-universal
120	            os: macos-latest
121	            args: --target universal-apple-darwin
122	            rust-targets: aarch64-apple-darwin,x86_64-apple-darwin
123	          - name: linux-x86_64
124	            os: ubuntu-22.04
125	            args: ""
126	            rust-targets: ""
127	          - name: windows-x86_64
128	            os: windows-latest
129	            args: ""
130	            rust-targets: ""
131	    steps:
132	      - uses: actions/checkout@v6
133	      - uses: jdx/mise-action@v4
134	        with:
135	          experimental: true
136	          install: true
137	          cache: true
138	      - name: add rust targets
139	        if: matrix.rust-targets != ''
140	        shell: bash
141	        env:
142	          RUST_TARGETS: ${{ matrix.rust-targets }}
143	        run: |
144	          IFS=',' read -r -a targets <<< "$RUST_TARGETS"
145	          for t in "${targets[@]}"; do rustup target add "$t"; done
146	      - uses: Swatinem/rust-cache@v2
147	        with:
148	          workspaces: "crates/orpheus-tauri/src-tauri -> target"
149	          shared-key: latest-tauri-${{ matrix.name }}
150	
151	      - uses: ./.github/actions/install-linux-deps
152	
153	      - run: mise run web:install
154	      - uses: tauri-apps/tauri-action@v0
155	        env:
156	          GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
157	        with:
158	          projectPath: crates/orpheus-tauri/src-tauri
159	          args: ${{ matrix.args }}
160	
161	      - name: collect tauri bundles
162	        shell: bash
163	        run: |
164	          set -euo pipefail
165	          mkdir -p tauri-output
166	          # Cargo workspace: bundles land under the workspace root `target/`.
167	          # Layout varies by target triple:
168	          #   native   -> target/release/bundle/<kind>/...
169	          #   crossed  -> target/<triple>/release/bundle/<kind>/...
170	          #               (e.g. target/universal-apple-darwin/release/bundle/...)
171	          # So search the whole target/ tree and filter by the
172	          # .../release/bundle/ path segment. Tauri v2 emits duplicates
173	          # (Orpheus_0.1.0_amd64.deb AND lowercase
174	          # orpheus_0.1.0_amd64.deb from the Cargo crate name); the
175	          # Orpheus_/Orpheus- name filter keeps only the productName-cased
176	          # ones.
177	          find target -type f -path "*/release/bundle/*" \
