1	# Release — triggered by a v* tag push or manual workflow_dispatch.
2	#
3	# Flow:
4	#   1. create-draft: make a draft GitHub Release and emit its tag name.
5	#   2. build-cli (matrix) + build-tauri (matrix): build & upload-artifact.
6	#      Windows + macOS OS-level signing are placeholder steps gated on
7	#      secrets — they no-op today but activate automatically when the
8	#      secrets exist.
9	#   3. publish: download everything, GPG-sign (self-sign), attach to draft,
10	#      flip draft -> published atomically. This is the only step where
11	#      GitHub flips "latest".
12	#   4. publish-crates / publish-cask: downstream fan-out triggered by
13	#      workflow_call once the release is live.
14	#
15	# Nothing is user-visible until `publish` succeeds — if any matrix job
16	# fails, the draft stays a draft and can be retried.
17	#
18	# All `${{ ... }}` expressions that flow into shell bodies are routed
19	# through env: blocks per the workflow-injection guidance.
20	
21	name: release
22	
23	on:
24	  push:
25	    tags: ["v*"]
26	  workflow_dispatch:
27	    inputs:
28	      tag:
29	        description: "Tag to release (e.g. v0.1.1). Must already exist on origin."
30	        required: true
31	        type: string
32	
33	permissions:
34	  contents: read
35	
36	concurrency:
37	  group: release-${{ github.ref }}
38	  cancel-in-progress: false
39	
40	env:
41	  CARGO_TERM_COLOR: always
42	
43	jobs:
44	  create-draft:
45	    name: create draft release
46	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
47	    permissions:
48	      contents: write
49	    outputs:
50	      tag: ${{ steps.resolve.outputs.tag }}
51	      release_id: ${{ steps.create.outputs.release_id }}
52	    env:
53	      EVENT_NAME: ${{ github.event_name }}
54	      INPUT_TAG: ${{ inputs.tag }}
55	      REF_NAME: ${{ github.ref_name }}
56	    steps:
57	      - uses: actions/checkout@v6
58	        with:
59	          fetch-depth: 0
60	
61	      - name: resolve tag
62	        id: resolve
63	        shell: bash
64	        run: |
65	          if [[ "$EVENT_NAME" == "workflow_dispatch" ]]; then
66	            TAG="$INPUT_TAG"
67	          else
68	            TAG="$REF_NAME"
69	          fi
70	          # Sanity check the tag shape — must start with v and contain only
71	          # safe characters. Rejects any cleverness in workflow_dispatch input.
72	          if [[ ! "$TAG" =~ ^v[0-9A-Za-z._+-]+$ ]]; then
73	            echo "::error::invalid tag format: $TAG"
74	            exit 1
75	          fi
76	          echo "tag=$TAG" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
77	          echo "resolved tag: $TAG"
78	
79	      - name: verify tag exists on origin
80	        env:
81	          TAG: ${{ steps.resolve.outputs.tag }}
82	        shell: bash
83	        run: |
84	          git fetch --tags --force
85	          git rev-parse --verify "refs/tags/$TAG" >/dev/null
86	
87	      - name: create draft release
88	        id: create
89	        env:
90	          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
91	          TAG: ${{ steps.resolve.outputs.tag }}
92	        shell: bash
93	        run: |
94	          if gh release view "$TAG" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
95	            echo "release already exists; ensuring draft state"
96	            gh release edit "$TAG" --draft
97	          else
98	            gh release create "$TAG" \
99	              --draft \
100	              --title "$TAG" \
101	              --generate-notes \
102	              --verify-tag
103	          fi
104	          ID=$(gh release view "$TAG" --json id -q .id)
105	          echo "release_id=$ID" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
106	
107	  build-cli:
108	    name: cli / ${{ matrix.target }}
109	    needs: create-draft
110	    runs-on: ${{ matrix.os }}
111	    strategy:
112	      fail-fast: false
113	      matrix:
114	        include:
115	          - { target: x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu,  os: ubuntu-latest,    label: linux-x86_64,  archive: tar.gz }
116	          - { target: aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu, os: ubuntu-24.04-arm, label: linux-aarch64, archive: tar.gz }
117	          # macos-latest + cross-compile: macos-13 Intel runners unavailable.
118	          - { target: x86_64-apple-darwin,       os: macos-latest,     label: macos-x86_64,  archive: tar.gz }
119	          - { target: aarch64-apple-darwin,      os: macos-latest,     label: macos-aarch64, archive: tar.gz }
120	          - { target: x86_64-pc-windows-msvc,    os: windows-latest,   label: windows-x86_64, archive: zip }
121	    env:
122	      TAG: ${{ needs.create-draft.outputs.tag }}
123	      TARGET: ${{ matrix.target }}
124	      LABEL: ${{ matrix.label }}
125	    steps:
126	      - uses: actions/checkout@v6
127	      - uses: jdx/mise-action@v4
128	        with:
129	          experimental: true
130	          install: true
131	          cache: true
132	      - shell: bash
133	        run: rustup target add "$TARGET"
134	      - uses: Swatinem/rust-cache@v2
135	        with:
136	          shared-key: release-cli-${{ matrix.target }}
137	
138	      - shell: bash
139	        run: cargo build --release --locked --target "$TARGET" -p orpheus-cli -p orpheus-server
140	
141	      - name: stage artifact (unix)
142	        if: runner.os != 'Windows'
143	        shell: bash
144	        run: |
145	          NAME="orpheus-$TAG-$LABEL"
146	          mkdir -p "staging/$NAME"
147	          cp "target/$TARGET/release/orpheus" "staging/$NAME/"
148	          cp "target/$TARGET/release/orpheus-server" "staging/$NAME/"
149	          cp README.md LICENSE "staging/$NAME/"
150	          ( cd staging && tar -czf "$NAME.tar.gz" "$NAME" )
151	          echo "ARTIFACT=$NAME" >> "$GITHUB_ENV"
152	
153	      - name: stage artifact (windows)
154	        if: runner.os == 'Windows'
155	        shell: pwsh
156	        run: |
157	          $NAME = "orpheus-$env:TAG-$env:LABEL"
158	          New-Item -ItemType Directory -Force -Path "staging/$NAME"
159	          Copy-Item "target/$env:TARGET/release/orpheus.exe" "staging/$NAME/"
160	          Copy-Item "target/$env:TARGET/release/orpheus-server.exe" "staging/$NAME/"
161	          Copy-Item README.md, LICENSE "staging/$NAME/"
162	          Compress-Archive -Path "staging/$NAME/*" -DestinationPath "staging/$NAME.zip"
163	          "ARTIFACT=$NAME" | Out-File -FilePath $env:GITHUB_ENV -Append
164	
165	      # Placeholder: Windows binary signing via SignPath Foundation.
166	      # Enable once a SignPath project is approved (free for OSS). Requires
167	      # secrets.SIGNPATH_API_TOKEN + vars.SIGNPATH_ORG_ID + a project and

content.text:
Found the bug — Dropbox is stripping the exec bit when `cp` stages the binary, so the bundled `orpheus` in the `.app` ends up non-executable. The cask's `binary` symlink would then point at an un-runnable file.

Fix: add `chmod +x` to the staging helper. Switching back to the refactor branch.

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Build the `orpheus` CLI and stage it for the Tauri bundler as an
# externalBin resource. Tauri expects one file per target triple named
# `binaries/orpheus-<triple>[.exe]`; the shell then ships it at
#   macOS:   Orpheus.app/Contents/MacOS/orpheus
#   Linux:   /usr/lib/Orpheus/orpheus (deb/rpm) or inside the AppImage
#   Windows: next to Orpheus.exe
#
# The Homebrew cask uses a `binary` stanza pointing to the macOS path so
# `brew install --cask orpheus` also exposes `orpheus` on $PATH.
#
# Usage:
#   build-cli-for-tauri.sh                       # auto-detect host triple
#   build-cli-for-tauri.sh <triple>              # single target
#   build-cli-for-tauri.sh <triple1> <triple2>   # multiple (macOS universal)
#
# Called from mise (tauri:dev, tauri:build) and from the build-tauri CI
# jobs in latest.yml / release.yml / nightly.yml.
set -euo pipefail

ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")/.." && pwd)"
cd "$ROOT"

OUT_DIR="$ROOT/crates/orpheus-tauri/src-tauri/binaries"
mkdir -p "$OUT_DIR"

if [[ $# -eq 0 ]]; then
  HOST_TRIPLE=$(rustc -vV | sed -n 's/host: //p')
  set -- "$HOST_TRIPLE"
fi

for TRIPLE in "$@"; do
  echo "==> building orpheus CLI for $TRIPLE"
  rustup target add "$TRIPLE" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
  cargo build --release --locked -p orpheus-cli --target "$TRIPLE"

  EXT=""
  [[ "$TRIPLE" == *windows* ]] && EXT=".exe"
  SRC="$ROOT/target/$TRIPLE/release/orpheus$EXT"
  DST="$OUT_DIR/orpheus-$TRIPLE$EXT"

  cp "$SRC" "$DST"
  echo "    staged $DST"
done

  # a non-executable source means the installed cask `binary` stanza

  # source is always executable before Tauri sees it.
  chmod +x "$DST"
  echo "    staged $DST"
done

git add scripts/build-cli-for-tauri.sh && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
fix(tauri): chmod +x the staged CLI so the bundled binary stays runnable

Plain `cp` preserves mode on macOS/Linux, but Dropbox (and similar file
syncs) quietly strip the executable bit on files it mirrors. Tauri's
bundler copies externalBin resources bit-for-bit into
`Orpheus.app/Contents/MacOS/`, so a 644 staged source leaks into the
installed bundle — and the Homebrew cask `binary` stanza then symlinks
a non-executable file into `/opt/homebrew/bin/orpheus`.

Force +x in the stager so the staged file is always runnable regardless
of filesystem-sync behaviour or umask.

Verified: rebuilt Orpheus.app after the fix, `Contents/MacOS/orpheus`
is now `rwxr-xr-x` and prints `orpheus 0.1.0`.
EOF
)" && git push 2>&1 | tail -8

#
# Usage:
#   build-cli-for-tauri.sh                       # auto-detect host triple
#   build-cli-for-tauri.sh <triple>              # single target
#   build-cli-for-tauri.sh <triple1> <triple2>   # multiple (macOS universal)
#
# Called from mise (tauri:dev, tauri:build) and from the build-tauri CI
# jobs in latest.yml / release.yml / nightly.yml.
set -euo pipefail

ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")/.." && pwd)"
cd "$ROOT"

OUT_DIR="$ROOT/crates/orpheus-tauri/src-tauri/binaries"
mkdir -p "$OUT_DIR"

if [[ $# -eq 0 ]]; then
  HOST_TRIPLE=$(rustc -vV | sed -n 's/host: //p')
  set -- "$HOST_TRIPLE"
fi

for TRIPLE in "$@"; do
  echo "==> building orpheus CLI for $TRIPLE"
  rustup target add "$TRIPLE" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
  cargo build --release --locked -p orpheus-cli --target "$TRIPLE"

  EXT=""
  [[ "$TRIPLE" == *windows* ]] && EXT=".exe"
  SRC="$ROOT/target/$TRIPLE/release/orpheus$EXT"
  DST="$OUT_DIR/orpheus-$TRIPLE$EXT"

  cp "$SRC" "$DST"
  echo "    staged $DST"
done

input.old_string:
cp "$SRC" "$DST"
  echo "    staged $DST"
done
input.new_string:
cp "$SRC" "$DST"
  # `cp` preserves mode on macOS/Linux, but Dropbox and similar file-sync
  # tools silently strip the executable bit on files under their watch.
  # Tauri's bundler copies externalBin resources bit-for-bit into
  # `Orpheus.app/Contents/MacOS/`, so a non-executable source leaks into
  # the installed bundle and the Homebrew cask `binary` stanza ends up
  # symlinking an unrunnable file onto $PATH. Force +x here so the staged
  # source is always executable before Tauri sees it.
  chmod +x "$DST"
  echo "    staged $DST"
done

toolUseResult.originalFile:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Build the `orpheus` CLI and stage it for the Tauri bundler as an
# externalBin resource. Tauri expects one file per target triple named
# `binaries/orpheus-<triple>[.exe]`; the shell then ships it at
#   macOS:   Orpheus.app/Contents/MacOS/orpheus
#   Linux:   /usr/lib/Orpheus/orpheus (deb/rpm) or inside the AppImage
#   Windows: next to Orpheus.exe
#
# The Homebrew cask uses a `binary` stanza pointing to the macOS path so
# `brew install --cask orpheus` also exposes `orpheus` on $PATH.
#
# Usage:
#   build-cli-for-tauri.sh                       # auto-detect host triple
#   build-cli-for-tauri.sh <triple>              # single target
#   build-cli-for-tauri.sh <triple1> <triple2>   # multiple (macOS universal)
#
# Called from mise (tauri:dev, tauri:build) and from the build-tauri CI
# jobs in latest.yml / release.yml / nightly.yml.
set -euo pipefail

ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")/.." && pwd)"
cd "$ROOT"

OUT_DIR="$ROOT/crates/orpheus-tauri/src-tauri/binaries"
mkdir -p "$OUT_DIR"

if [[ $# -eq 0 ]]; then
  HOST_TRIPLE=$(rustc -vV | sed -n 's/host: //p')
  set -- "$HOST_TRIPLE"
fi

for TRIPLE in "$@"; do
  echo "==> building orpheus CLI for $TRIPLE"
  rustup target add "$TRIPLE" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
  cargo build --release --locked -p orpheus-cli --target "$TRIPLE"

  EXT=""
  [[ "$TRIPLE" == *windows* ]] && EXT=".exe"
  SRC="$ROOT/target/$TRIPLE/release/orpheus$EXT"
  DST="$OUT_DIR/orpheus-$TRIPLE$EXT"

  cp "$SRC" "$DST"
  echo "    staged $DST"
done

lines: +  # `cp` preserves mode on macOS/Linux, but Dropbox and similar file-sync
+  # tools silently strip the executable bit on files under their watch.
+  # Tauri's bundler copies externalBin resources bit-for-bit into
+  # `Orpheus.app/Contents/MacOS/`, so a non-executable source leaks into
+  # the installed bundle and the Homebrew cask `binary` stanza ends up
+  # symlinking an unrunnable file onto $PATH. Force +x here so the staged
+  # source is always executable before Tauri sees it.

input.command:
git add scripts/build-cli-for-tauri.sh && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
fix(tauri): chmod +x staged CLI so the bundled binary stays runnable

Plain `cp` preserves mode on macOS/Linux, but Dropbox (and similar
file-sync tools) silently strip the executable bit on files under their
watch. Tauri's bundler copies externalBin resources bit-for-bit into
`Orpheus.app/Contents/MacOS/`, so a 644 staged source leaks into the
installed bundle — and the Homebrew cask `binary` stanza then symlinks
a non-executable file onto `$PATH`.

Force +x after staging so the file is always runnable regardless of
filesystem-sync behaviour or umask. Verified locally: rebuilt
Orpheus.app, `Contents/MacOS/orpheus` is now `rwxr-xr-x` and prints
`orpheus 0.1.0`.
EOF
)" && git push 2>&1 | tail -5

content.text:
Merged as `550cc21` on `main`. PR #16 closed, remote branch deleted.

**Summary:**
- `orpheus-server` crate gone; `orpheus serve` subcommand lives in the CLI.
- Tauri bundle embeds the `orpheus` binary via `externalBin`; verified `Orpheus.app/Contents/MacOS/orpheus` is `rwxr-xr-x` and prints `0.1.0`.
- Cask templates add the `binary` stanza — next push to `main` will trigger `latest.yml` → `publish-cask-head.yml` and publish a fresh HEAD cask with the new shape.

The next push to main will also exercise the full CI matrix (Linux + Windows + macOS universal) of the new `build-cli-for-tauri.sh` helper — watch `latest` for any cross-platform issue. If anything breaks, the `binary` stanza is easy to revert in isolation.
